title: FrankenLean program root: drop-in native-Rust Lean 4 toolchain (plan §0)
description:
Program root for FrankenLean (franken_lean, crate prefix fln-): a ground-up, native-Rust reimplementation of the ENTIRE Lean 4 toolchain — parser, macro engine, elaborator, unifier, instance engine, tactic framework, simp + decision procedures, trusted kernel, compiler, VM, runtime/ABI twin, module codec, build fabric, and language server — that is a drop-in replacement at six binary surfaces (source language, .olean read AND write, lean_object C ABI, .ilean/artifact chain, the Lean.* Meta API via the Native Mirror, LSP + $/lean/* + CLI).

Constitutional laws (never violate): the Oracle-Only Law D8 (upstream implementation code NEVER executes as a component; the Reference lives only inside the Tribunal as differential oracle, fixture/census mine, and Init/Std source input); closed dependency universe (std + pinned nightly + FrankenSuite: asupersync, frankensqlite, franken_networkx, frankensearch, frankentui, franken_markdown, fastmcp_rust, atp); zero mandatory external tools (cc and git optional-only per D2); forbid(unsafe) outside fln-unsafe-abi/-region/-jit; kernel covenant <= 12 KLOC with FL-INV-02; determinism as invariant FL-INV-01; claims have types (D7, L0-L4 / R0-R5 evidence axes).

Success = R4 (drop-in epoch replacement) for the pinned epoch (Reference leanprover/lean4 v4.32.0; Corpus mathlib4 at compatible commit, both pinned in SUITE.lock) on the declared platform matrix, via gates G0..G6 (§22). The single source of truth is COMPREHENSIVE_PLAN_FOR_THE_DESIGN_OF_FRANKEN_LEAN.md; this bead tree is its executable projection. Sequencing bends to dependency; ambition does not bend to sequencing.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. title: G0: The Laws of the Machine — pre-construction spike gate (§22.1)
description:
Gate G0 "The Laws of the Machine" (§22.1): ten compile-tested spikes and executed decisions that retire the load-bearing unknowns BEFORE any W2-W12 interface freezes. No later gate may begin with its unknowns unresolved (Declaration of Intent commitment 7).

The spikes price the central bets early: ABI resurrection of a real mathlib .olean (feeds Marrow/Grimoire layout methodology), independent kernel check vs lean4checker, Golem execution of real user-class compiled code on ABI values, hygiene/syntax fidelity on macro-heavy corpus files, byte-identical olean re-emit (FL-INV-04 feasibility), heartbeat/fuel-parity calibration, the defeq tail (twenty nastiest Corpus problems on the K2 memoization design), the Mirror-facade viability spike (Bet B2 priced before interfaces freeze), the instrumented-oracle spike (golden decision traces — mitigation for the R2 unifier-fidelity risk), and the full dependency-closure audit with SUITE.lock.

Exit criteria: every spike green or its finding folded into an amended interface decision recorded in the plan (§25 decision log); open questions OQ-1/OQ-2/OQ-3 resolved or explicitly owned by a named workstream. Each spike bead states its own acceptance test. This epic closes only when all ten spike beads close.

title: W1: Substrate & Contracts — fln-core, extraction law, Tribunal bootstrap, baselines
description:
Workstream W1 "Substrate & Contracts" (§22.2): the foundation everything else compiles against, plus the conformance apparatus bootstrapped FIRST so every later workstream is born differential.

Scope: fln-core (names, universe levels, Expr constructor inventory with cached hashes / loose-bvar ranges / has-fvar+has-mvar flags exactly as the Reference exposes them — §1.1; literals, options, byte positions); fln-hash (owned BLAKE3-class hashing, domain-separation registry, canonical serialization schemas); the mechanical contract-extraction toolchain (ABI_CONTRACT.md, OLEAN_CONTRACT.md, extern + builtin censuses per Appendices B/C — extracted from the pin by checked-in scripts, never hand-transcribed, CI re-extracts and fails on drift, Rule D5/D9); the Tribunal rig + differential harness against the Reference binaries (epoch laboratory: hashed official archives, CLI/LSP transcripts, quirk ledgers, per-platform reproduction commands — §18); the W1 baseline performance report of the Reference at the pin on both pinned bare-metal profiles (§19.1 — gate numbers are ratified against it); the dependency-closure allowlist + SUITE.lock ceremony (D1); windows functional CI; G0 coordination.

Depends on nothing; everything depends on it. Exit: contracts generated and CI-locked, Tribunal executes a Reference-vs-Reference smoke differential, baseline report checked in, closure audit green.

title: W2: Marrow & Grimoire — ABI twin, object model, environment, olean codec
description:
Workstream W2 "Marrow & Grimoire" (§6, §7): the runtime/ABI twin and the environment/module codec — the load-bearing act of drop-in compatibility and the spine of gate G1.

Marrow: the Reference object model implemented exactly in Rust under the lean_* exported names (header packing, tag bytes, scalar-field packing, odd-tagged-pointer small Nats, tri-state RC discipline), with layout constants compiled from the GENERATED contract tables of §6.5 — never transcribed. Two heaps, one membrane (§6.1b): CompatHeap (exact ABI layouts, membrane-only allocation, unsafe confined to fln-unsafe-abi) vs NativeHeap (typed asupersync region handles for toolchain-internal structures); values cross only through generated converters; no raw Compat pointer ever enters the kernel (D22). Owned size-classed allocator with the heartbeat hook (fuel parity lives here). Compacted regions: construction, mmap-load, relocation — deliberately the SAME code path the olean codec uses.

Grimoire: the environment as persistent HAMT-style maps with O(1) snapshots and logical roots (§7.1); total contractual interning with alpha-canonical digest lane (§7.1b); olean codec read (three validation levels) and faithful byte-identical write (FL-INV-04); .ilean codec; olean-next frontier lane; import-at-daemon-scale shared heap (§7.5). Effects ride asupersync behind Reference IO/Task semantics (§6.7). fln-libm owned deterministic transcendentals (§6.8). Exit: G1 spine ready — real mathlib oleans resurrect, re-emit byte-identically, and load into the Reference.

title: W3: Crucible & Bignum — dual-engine kernel, independent checker, receipts
description:
Workstream W3 "Crucible & Bignum" (§8): the trusted kernel — the one subsystem where FrankenLean volunteers to be outnumbered — plus the owned arithmetic beneath it.

Deliverable order is constitutional: KERNEL_CONTRACT.md (the Judgment Specification, §8.1b) comes FIRST — a rule-by-rule account of the implemented type theory, each rule anchored to Reference source lines, against which both engines and the Tribunal generators are built. Then: K1 certified small-step evaluator (auditability-first, the code a skeptical logician reads in an afternoon); K2 NbE accelerator (memoized whnf keyed on term-hash x transparency, lazy delta by height) cross-checked against K1 continuously; fln-checker, a deliberately DIFFERENT independent implementation (eager normalization, own decoder over canonical wire objects, no unsafe-membrane dependency, WASM-clean, non-sharing proven structurally); fln-bignum (limb-based, ABI-layout-matching, thresholds tuned on the kernel-reduction corpus, PG-K gate vs GMP); typestate admission (UnvalidatedExpr -> ScopedExpr -> CheckedExpr, DeclarationEnvelope, four-way verdict incl. Inconclusive per FL-INV-07); typed resource budgets (§8.2c); fuel parity (§8.5); certificates + receipts + transparency log (§8.6); consensus policies where disagreement halts and never outvotes (§8.3c).

Covenant: fln-kernel <= 12 KLOC, forbid(unsafe), deps = fln-bignum + read-only term views + core/alloc only, one exported authority (check), CI-counted. Divergence law: kernel disagreement with the Reference at the pin is release-blocking; soundness beats bug-parity (D23). Exit: gate G1 — every mathlib olean re-checked with zero acceptance divergence, PG-1 met.

title: W4: Quill — parser, Syntax/SourceInfo, hygiene, macro transactions
description:
Workstream W4 "Quill" (§9): the parser and macro engine — the reimplementation graveyard, visited early with flowers (spike G0-4).

Scope: token tables + Pratt-precedence engine with the Reference category/leading/trailing model; Syntax trees with identical node kinds, atom/ident/node structure, SourceInfo discipline (original vs synthetic, whitespace attachment, UTF-8 byte positions — wire-visible, therefore conformance surface); the full extensibility contract (parsing interleaves with elaboration; commands install syntax/macros visible immediately; precedence interactions match). Internals: lossless rope + persistent green tree with red views; origin sets per node projected to the single-primary-span convention at the wire; GrammarEpoch content-hashing of the active grammar state with memoization keyed on (position, category, precedence, epoch); typed parser-registration effect summaries (an OpaqueParserEffect forces the conservative suffix barrier). Hygiene: macro scopes exactly as the Reference implements them; generated identifiers derive from stable expansion paths, never thread timing; macro expansion as a memoizable transaction with declared capability reads (§9.2). Sound-mode improvements (§9.3): specified error recovery (recovery may add diagnostics, never change acceptance; RecoveredSyntax law), sub-command incremental relex/reparse, provenance into Palimpsest.

Exit: hygiene fidelity rig green on the macro-heavy corpus; syntax trees, positions, and hygiene observables byte-agree with the Reference across C0-C2 fixtures.

title: W5: Golem — FIR pipeline, FLBC interpreter, intrinsic table
description:
Workstream W5 "Golem" (§11): the metaprogram compiler and VM — the vehicle for ALL user-class Lean code (mathlib tactics, project macros, lakefiles, #eval, plugins) under the Oracle-Only Law (upstream implementation never rides it; the only Lean it executes is user code and source-elaborated library code per §4.3).

Pipeline: elaborated terms -> FIR (ANF/LCNF-class IR keeping the Reference-established disciplines: specialization, simplification, closure conversion, explicit boxing, RC insertion with borrow inference + constructor-reuse/FBIP — performance-observable, the ecosystem tuned against it) -> FLBC register bytecode whose values ARE Marrow ABI objects (the quiet masterstroke: zero conversion across interpreted code, Iron-compiled code, Reference-built plugins, and the Mirror facade — one calling convention for the whole membrane). Every pass is a pure ValidatedIr -> (ValidatedIr, PassWitness) transformation with validators; the RC-insertion ownership validator is simpler than the optimizer it audits. Interpreter: threaded dispatch, inline caches, allocation through Marrow so heartbeats count identically, Reference semantics for panics/partial/stack/checkSystem (PG-7 floor: >= 3x Reference interpreter on the tactic micro-corpus). Intrinsic table generated from the @[extern] census (Appendix C). Initialization ordering + capability-scoped effects incl. --pure-elab audit mode (§11.5). native_decide staged repair Tier 1: replay receipts (§11.6).

Exit: gate G2 "The Golem Wakes" — user-class code runs with #eval/IO/Task parity, Reference-built plugins load and run, intrinsic census fully populated with fixtures.

title: W6: Athanor, Synod & the Mirror — elaborator, instances, scheduler, facade
description:
Workstream W6 "Athanor, Synod & the Native Mirror" (§10, §4.3): the elaborator — the program-defining workstream carrying the two Critical risks (R1 Meta-API dependence, R2 unifier fidelity tail).

Scope: the monadic tower semantics (CoreM/MetaM/TermElabM/TacticM state shapes) as NativeHeap structures with explicit ElabTxn transactions (five outcomes, leak-audited rollback) and an explicit metavariable dependency graph with targeted wake-up; unification/defeq mirroring the Reference approximation ladder EXACTLY, implemented against the instrumented-oracle golden decision traces (§18.3) — the fidelity burden is entirely ours (no hosted fallback exists); term elaboration (coercions, binders, structure instances, match compilation matching generated-name conventions — metaprograms pattern-match on them, they are API; deriving; recursion elaborators lowering to exactly the kernel-visible constructions); Synod instance resolution (Reference semantics — priorities, outParam/semiOutParam, defaults, tabling — behind an indexed relational engine emitting SearchCards + TerminationCards); the NATIVE tactic framework (goal management, evalTactic dispatch, core tactic set behind Mirror facade rows); the deterministic dataflow scheduler (Bet B4: speculative parallel elaboration, canonical-order commit, effect summaries validated by perturbation — FL-INV-01 by construction); the builtin census + generated Mirror facade v1 (census totality: no unclassified symbol, ever — R1 mitigation).

Exit: gate G3 "The Mirror Holds" — Init/Std elaborate end-to-end from source with T2 identity, stdlib double-elaboration fixpoint bit-stable, metaprogram-corpus rig green at declared L-levels, ORACLE_FALLBACK compile-out check passes.

title: W7: Anvil & Verdict — simp core, rewrite indexes, owned SAT, grind
description:
Workstream W7 "Anvil & Verdict" (§12): rewrite and decision engines under the single constitutional rule FL-INV-06 — every engine is untrusted; every output enters the environment only through a kernel-checked artifact. That rule buys unlimited freedom below it.

Scope: simp faithful core (simp-set discipline, discrimination-tree retrieval, congruence interplay, dsimp/simp-only behaviors) implemented natively end-to-end behind obs-class Mirror facade rows, fidelity-tested against Corpus simp-heavy files + instrumented-oracle rewrite traces; compiled per-library rewrite indexes shipped as content-addressed Ledger artifacts (staleness impossible by construction); norm_num/omega native cores (fln-bignum-backed, certificate-producing, kernel-replayed); Verdict — owned CDCL SAT (watched literals, VSIDS/CHB, modest honest inprocessing) with OWNED proof logging and an OWNED independent certificate checker so bv_decide sheds the external CaDiCaL TCB (PG-9: 100% agreement, wall <= 2x Reference at G5, ratcheting); grind reimplemented natively (congruence closure, e-matching, theory hooks) entering at L1/L2 and ratcheting to L3 on the grind corpus by G4 — its semantics still move upstream, the census pins the coupling per epoch; e-graph saturation lane (frontier; honest scoping — conditional rewrites and binder-crossing rules restrict the eligible subset; completeness fallback to the faithful core; PG-A measured, not assumed); speculative tactic portfolios (frontier). New lanes promote only by shadow-run matrix protocol (§12.6/§18.9) with auto-demotion on breach.

title: W8: Ledger & Lake — content-addressed build fabric, invalidation law, cache
description:
Workstream W8 "Ledger & Lake" (§13): the build fabric (Bet B1) — the environment as a Merkle DAG of content-addressed declarations; builds as memoized queries; the ecosystem quality-of-life complaint #1 (file-granular invalidation) deleted.

Scope: Ledger records (decl_hash over name, elaborated statement, value class, direct dep hashes, options-that-matter, epoch, mode) generalized to demand nodes across five planes (source/language/trust/build/service) with retention tiers T0-T4; the incremental law (§13.2): true-dependency-cone invalidation on recorded edges, early cutoff (unchanged output digest stops the cascade; statement-hash vs value-hash separation), and the published, fixture-tested, perturbation-validated invalidation matrix per change class with conservative fallbacks (FL-INV-05: anything not proven irrelevant is relevant); the Lake surface (lakefile.lean executes on Golem — the config DSL is user code, so Golem IS the compatibility mechanism; lakefile.toml fast path; require via D2 git; exit codes and --json pinned; fln build explain shows Reference-decision vs native-decision); cache federation (CAS sync over atp multi-donor fountain-coded with HTTPS fallback; remote entries are untrusted until validated — malformed entry = miss + security event; fln cache shim preserves community muscle memory); epoch-stable caching + bridges (frontier, allowlisted, separately gated) + the migration autopilot (§13.6); watch mode + distributed elaboration (results re-verified by local Crucible — distribution without distributed trust).

Exit feeds gates G4 (mixed-build matrix) and G6 (PG-3 cone, PG-10 cache).

title: W9: Lantern & Palimpsest — daemon server, snapshots, causal proof graph
description:
Workstream W9 "Lantern & Palimpsest" (§14, §15): the single-daemon server behind the unchanged wire dialect, and the provenance substrate that makes the system explain itself.

Lantern: LSP + $/lean/* byte-compatible (plainGoal, plainTermGoal, rpc/* sessions, waitForDiagnostics, semantic tokens, widget delivery) — vscode-lean4 connects unmodified; one daemon, one shared immutable import heap (kills the ~60s x N-workers x GBs multiplication; PG-4/PG-6); snapshot machinery: immutable DocumentSnapshots with publication generations (stale-diagnostics race structurally impossible), sub-command incrementality from the finest enclosing checkpoint, cancel-correct under typing bursts, stable-identity diagnostics dedup, idempotent precondition-checked code actions, progressive proof-state negotiation, per-root epoch capsules, session replay bundles (server bugs become deterministic objects); the machine session API — fork a proof state, apply a tactic, read goals, O(1) per branch, thousands of branches per daemon (§14.4, the number that embarrasses the 18-735s per-branch literature).

Palimpsest: always-on ring-buffer traces + opt-in archives (frankensqlite); the causal proof graph (Bet B7) with typed edge families and completeness classes — ONLY Complete/Conservative edges may drive invalidation or trust (the welding law); unification forensics (minimal disagreement), instance why-not trees, semantic blame slices; semantic diff levels + semantic merge (blocks silent semantic conflicts Git cannot see; nothing merges around the kernel); fragility signals (advisory, D3); time travel, fln replay, repro bundles; build forensics as SQL.

Exit: gate G5 — vscode-lean4 smoke matrix green, PG-4/5/6/L met, session API live.

title: W10: Backends & ABI doors — Iron native backend, JIT, C parity, outbound plugins
description:
Workstream W10 "Backends & ABI doors" (§17.3, §17.4, §11.3): the sovereign compilation path and the bidirectional plugin promise.

Scope: Iron static backend — FLBC to native objects/executables (x86-64, aarch64 first) with NO LLVM, no cranelift, no cc: the owned baseline emitter grown a static mode; lake build of executables with zero external toolchain (the G6 headline). Iron-JIT (frontier, standard-mode only, fln-unsafe-jit): baseline template JIT with inline caches burned in; results bit-identical to the interpreter by construction, spot-checked by a standing dual-run rig; certified builds never enable it. --backend c parity generator: emit C in the Reference idiom and drive the USER toolchain exactly as upstream leanc does (D2 optional tool #1) — parity apparatus and exotic-target escape hatch, never the anchor. Both backends dual-run governed: Iron pinned to interpreter results, interpreter pinned to Reference observable evaluation semantics on the differential corpus. ABI door outbound (§17.4, the harder direction, staged to G5): FrankenLean-built plugins loading into the Reference host, per-platform Tribunal matrix; inbound (Reference plugins into FrankenLean) lands earlier under W2/G2. Risk R5 honesty: outbound tiers per platform in the Parity Ledger rather than blocking G5.

title: W11: Leapfrog surfaces — Bloodhound, Folio, Envoy, terminal InfoView, WASM Judge
description:
Workstream W11 "Leapfrog surfaces" (§16): the products that make FrankenLean more than parity — each strictly downstream of the trust core, each degradable without touching the trust story.

Bloodhound (fln-hound, frankensearch): in-toolchain hybrid lexical+semantic search over the live environment, indexed incrementally at Ledger commit; exact?/apply?-class premise retrieval as a candidate generator whose candidates are VERIFIED by actual elaboration (ranking is UX, proofs are proofs); CandidateCards in three grades (initial/refined/verified); proof repair consuming the causal graph (diff environments, find earliest failed constraint cell, hole the blame slice, search replacements, validate through the full pipeline, emit patch transaction with rollback — the migration autopilot engine); counterfactual analysis and minimization along declared objective vectors. Folio (fln-doc, franken_markdown+fmd-math): doc-gen4-surface-compatible docs as an incremental Ledger facet — native HTML/PDF, native math, receipt-bound pages that go stale honestly rather than lying. Envoy (fln-mcp, fastmcp_rust): the agent door — ProofStateSnapshot as the round-trippable session primitive; budgeted, macaroon-scoped tools (goal_state, apply_tactic, search_lemmas, check_term, eval, get_certificate, ledger_query, trace_query); transactional multi-agent orchestration (patch proposals, never direct trusted mutations; mission receipts with rollback roots). Terminal InfoView (fln goals over frankentui). The WASM Judge (§16.4b): K1 + fln-checker compiled to WASM — sealed-capsule verification in a browser tab, the public face of the trust story.

title: W12: Distribution & Epochs — releases, elan layout, epoch ratchet, Witness matrix
description:
Workstream W12 "Distribution & Epochs" (§22.5, §13.6, §17.1): shipping the sovereign toolchain and keeping it current against a monthly upstream without ever lying about parity.

Scope: static binaries per platform (lean, leanc, lake, fln) with the elan-compatible release layout so a lean-toolchain line can name a FrankenLean toolchain and everything downstream just works; the install script; version-line parse compatibility with a machine-readable implementation field + fln identity --json (compatibility never becomes deception, §17.1); reproducible releases (built twice in isolated builders and byte-compared; --reproducible certified matrix; windows bit-certification as a separate declared decision, D17); the epoch-advance ratchet as tooling (the mechanical 8 steps of §22.5: fetch+hash, regenerate censuses on every platform, run corpus differentials, machine-diff all public surfaces, regenerate facade stubs + open migration beads per moved symbol, close-or-classify every divergence — unclassified BLOCKS the epoch claim, run unchanged downstream corpus under both epochs, publish capsule + evidence bundle + migration report); side-by-side epoch installs; the public lag dashboard (target: new stable reaches R3 in four weeks, R4 next cycle); the Witness claim-to-proof matrix + documentation CI (§20.4: docs wording may never exceed evidence state; a benchmark is OBSERVED, never PROVEN).

title: G0-1: ABI resurrection spike — parse a real mathlib .olean via extracted contracts
description:
G0-1 ABI resurrection spike (§22.1-1, feeds §6/§7.2): parse a REAL mathlib .olean at the pin with a prototype Marrow/Grimoire reader — header (magic, version, base address), region relocation, ModuleData traversal — walk every constant and every environment-extension entry, and validate object-graph integrity against the extracted contract tables (not against hand-written constants!).

Why this is first: the entire drop-in promise rests on the object model and the region format being EXACTLY right, and on the extraction methodology (checked-in scripts parsing the pinned lean.h and runtime layout sources -> generated Rust constants module) being viable before anything depends on it. A layout truth discovered late invalidates every downstream interface.

Acceptance: (a) a chosen set of real mathlib oleans (include at least one with heavy environment-extension payloads, e.g. simp-set extensions) fully traversed with zero integrity faults; (b) every field decoded is traceable to a generated contract row with provenance; (c) unknown/opaque extension payloads preserved losslessly and flagged, never guessed; (d) a written findings memo enumerating every layout subtlety discovered (tag values, scalar packing corner cases, relocation quirks) folded into OLEAN_CONTRACT.md / ABI_CONTRACT.md. Tests: fixture corpus checked into the Tribunal (C3 family seed) with a round-trip traversal harness and hostile-input fuzz smoke (bounds, tags, reachability) under resource budgets.

title: G0-2: Independent-check spike — prototype kernel vs lean4checker on real modules
description:
G0-2 Independent-check spike (§22.1-2, feeds §8): a prototype Crucible checks a nontrivial upstream module from its olean — statements AND proofs — with verdicts diffed against lean4checker on the same input.

This is where the kernel judgment inventory (§1.2, Appendix A) meets reality: typing rules for every constructor, universe normalization with the imax collapse, defeq (beta, eta for functions and structures, delta by height, zeta, iota, proof irrelevance, Quot computation, Nat/String literal rules), inductive validation (strict positivity, nested-to-mutual, K-like elimination), structure eta, environment rules. The spike does not need speed (K2 comes later) — it needs to prove the rules are correctly understood and that the differential harness against a foreign checker is practical.

Acceptance: (a) chosen module set (progressively: a small Init module, a Std module, one mathlib file with defeq-heavy content) checks with verdicts identical to lean4checker; (b) any disagreement triaged to root cause — each becomes a fixture and, if upstream-vs-theory, is pre-classified under the §8.2 soundness-beats-bug-parity law; (c) fuel/heartbeat hooks stubbed but counted, proving the accounting seam exists. Tests: the diff harness itself lands in fln-conformance as the seed of the standing kernel differential rig (§8.7) and runs in CI on the fixture set from this spike onward.

title: G0-3: Golem execution spike — user-class code + real plugin on ABI-valued VM
description:
G0-3 Golem execution spike (§22.1-3, feeds §11): execute REAL user-class compiled Lean code end-to-end on the prototype VM — an #eval/IO/Task parity micro-corpus compiled from source by the prototype pipeline, plus loading a precompiled Reference-built plugin at the pin — through the prototype intrinsic table operating on ABI values.

The claim being priced: FLBC values ARE Marrow ABI objects, so interpreted code, native intrinsics, and Reference-built plugins share one calling convention with zero conversion. If that identity does not hold in practice (hidden boxing mismatches, RC discipline drift at the call boundary, initializer ordering surprises), the whole membrane design must be amended BEFORE W5/W6 freeze interfaces.

Acceptance: (a) the parity micro-corpus (pure computation, Nat/String/Array ops, closures, IO println/file ops, Task spawn/bind/get ordering, panics, partial non-termination guard) produces observably identical results to the Reference running the same corpus; (b) a real precompiled plugin dlopens, resolves lean_* imports against prototype Marrow exports, runs its initializers in Reference order, and its functions are callable from interpreted code; (c) RC balance verified (no leak, no double-free) under the debug ownership shadow. Tests: the corpus + plugin harness become the seed of the C4 native-ABI probe family and the PG-7 interpreter benchmark baseline.

title: G0-4: Hygiene & syntax fidelity spike — macro-heavy corpus tree/position diff
description:
G0-4 Hygiene & syntax fidelity spike (§22.1-4, feeds §9): parse a curated set of macro-heavy Corpus files and compare Syntax trees, byte positions, and hygiene observables against the Reference (driven through its own frontend under instrumentation in the Tribunal sandbox).

Hygiene is the classic reimplementation graveyard: macro scopes appended to names, quotation/antiquotation semantics, macro_rules matching, and the parse/elaborate interleaving (a command can install a parser used three lines later) are all API surface that metaprograms and diagnostics observe. This spike visits the graveyard early. Curate files whose MEANING depends on hygiene corner cases: scope-shadowing across macro expansion, antiquotation splices, syntax categories with custom precedence, notation-priority interactions, auto-bound implicit capture.

Acceptance: (a) node kinds, atom/ident structure, SourceInfo (original vs synthetic, whitespace attachment, UTF-8 byte positions) byte-agree on the curated set; (b) hygiene-decorated generated names agree under the stable-expansion-path derivation; (c) every divergence triaged: fidelity defect (fix) or undocumented-upstream-behavior (fixture + upstream report + Behavior Note candidate); (d) findings feed the fln-syntax / fln-parse interface freeze. Tests: the curated set becomes the standing hygiene fidelity rig in fln-conformance, run per-commit.

title: G0-5: Byte-identical olean re-emit spike — enumerate and pin serialization freedoms
description:
G0-5 Byte-identical re-emit spike (§22.1-5, feeds §7.3): read a set of Reference-produced oleans, rebuild the compacted region from the decoded object graph, and byte-diff the output against the original. Then the harder direction: elaborate a module fresh (prototype path) and match the Reference emission for the deterministic subset.

This is the feasibility proof for FL-INV-04 (codec fidelity: read-then-write is byte identity in faithful mode) and directly prices risk R3 (byte-exact write over-constrained by hidden emission nondeterminism). Every serialization freedom found — object traversal order, region layout choices, padding, interning decisions — must be ENUMERATED and pinned with a registered CGSE policy, or (if genuinely nondeterministic upstream) documented as the scoped-down law of R3 (load-compatible + canonical write with the byte law kept for read-then-write).

Acceptance: (a) read->rebuild->bytediff == identical for the fixture set; (b) an exhaustive written enumeration of serialization freedoms with a pinned policy per row; (c) a verdict memo: FL-INV-04 stands as stated, or the R3 fallback scope is invoked with exact wording for the Parity Ledger row. Tests: the round-trip harness becomes the standing codec rig (§18.2 codec rigs) and the FL-INV-04 CI gate at PG-8.

title: G0-6: Fuel-parity spike — calibrate the heartbeat law tick-for-tick
description:
G0-6 Fuel-parity spike (§22.1-6, feeds §4.2/§6.3/§8.5): calibrate the heartbeat-counting law — upstream counts heartbeats in allocation-linked units (maxHeartbeats default 200000, units of thousands) — on an instrumented corpus until faithful-mode timeout behavior matches the Reference tick-for-tick, and document any irreducible divergence class.

Why it matters: determinism of FAILURE is part of determinism. Mathlib and downstream CI depend on maxHeartbeats/maxRecDepth limits firing on the same inputs; a reimplementation that times out differently silently changes which proofs are accepted in practice. The heartbeat hook lives in the owned allocator (§6.3), which is why the allocator is not an off-the-shelf component. This spike also prices risk R11 (heartbeat parity chaotic at fine grain) and resolves OQ-2 (the achievable tolerance class).

Acceptance: (a) an instrumented corpus where Reference heartbeat consumption is captured per declaration (instrumented-oracle apparatus); (b) prototype counting law reproduces timeout/no-timeout verdicts on every corpus entry, with tick deltas quantified; (c) a declared tolerance class (exact / bounded-drift / law-level parity per BN-08) ratified into the plan; (d) maxRecDepth accounting verified on deep-recursion fixtures. Tests: fuel-parity fixtures join the faithful-mode differential rig; any future allocator change must keep them green.

title: G0-7: Defeq-tail spike — the twenty nastiest Corpus whnf problems on K2 design
description:
G0-7 Defeq-tail spike (§22.1-7, feeds §10.2/§8.3): collect the twenty nastiest definitional-equality / whnf problems in the Corpus (the pathological tail that dominates elaboration wall time) and time them on the K2 NbE-accelerator design — memoized whnf keyed on (term-hash, transparency), lazy delta with height comparison, environment-aware closures.

Why: PG-2 (full-corpus elaboration <= 0.5x Reference) is impossible if the defeq tail does not fall; and the cache keying / lazy-delta strategy must be DECIDED before Athanor (W6) freezes against it. This spike turns the central performance bet from hope into measurement. Candidate problems: mathlib instances with deep unfolding chains, Fin/Nat computation-heavy defeq, structure-eta-heavy goals, whnf under binders with transparency flips.

Acceptance: (a) the twenty problems extracted as standalone fixtures with Reference timings captured on the pinned baseline machine; (b) K2 prototype timings within the envelope the PG-2 arithmetic requires (document the model: tail share of total wall x speedup needed); (c) the cache-keying and delta-ordering design ratified or amended in writing; (d) alpha-canonical digest sharing (§7.1b) validated as the cache-key normalization. Tests: the twenty fixtures become a named benchmark suite with per-commit regression tracking (tails gate, not just medians — §19).

title: G0-8: Mirror-facade viability spike — census + stubs vs real mathlib tactics
description:
G0-8 Mirror-facade viability spike (§22.1-8, feeds §4.3/§18.2): generate the builtin census and facade stubs for a metaprogram-heavy slice (Lean.Meta core + the tactic-framework entry points); compile a curated set of REAL mathlib tactic files against the facade with native services stubbed at prototype depth; enumerate every API whose semantics resist native backing and assign each an honest L-level.

This is the plan CENTRAL bet (B2) priced before interfaces freeze, and the direct mitigation of Critical risk R1 (Meta-API dependence effectively unbounded — packages compile against Lean.* internals that may expose object identity or allocation order the native facade cannot reproduce). The census partition (toolchain-api native / library-code source-elaborated / user-facing-data) must prove TOTAL on the slice: no unclassified symbol, ever — a symbol the census cannot classify blocks the L-level, never gets guessed.

Acceptance: (a) census extraction runs mechanically over the pinned Init/Std/Lean sources for the slice and emits symbol, signature, telescope, effect class, partition class; (b) generated facade stubs compile against real mathlib tactic code; (c) a written resistance list: every symbol whose observable semantics leak implementation detail, each with equivalence class (bit/obs/perf), honest L-level, and Behavior Note candidate; (d) the ordered closure plan for the facade L-level ratchet (resolves OQ-3). Tests: the slice + tactic files seed the metaprogram-corpus rig (§18.2 Mirror conformance).

title: G0-9: Instrumented-oracle spike — golden decision traces schema, volume, replay rig
description:
G0-9 Instrumented-oracle spike (§22.1-9, feeds §18.3): patch the Reference IN THE TRIBUNAL SANDBOX (build-time-only, test-only, never shipped, never linked — D8) to dump decision traces at the points our native machinery must match — unifier approximation choices, postponements, unfoldings and their order, instance-search candidate orders and outcomes, macro expansions, simp rewrite firings, heartbeat consumption — on a pilot corpus, and prove the trace schema, volume, and replay-rig design.

Why this exists: under the Oracle-Only Law there is NO hosted upstream engine to fall back to, so the unifier/instance/simp fidelity burden (Critical risk R2) is carried entirely by evidence. Golden decision traces at Corpus scale are the strongest available mitigation: Athanor, Synod, and Anvil are implemented AGAINST the traces, not against vibes, and trace-replay rigs run continuously thereafter.

Acceptance: (a) the patched-oracle build recipe is scripted, hashed, and reproducible in the sandbox; (b) trace schema versioned and validated (typed events, source anchors, stable ids); (c) volume measured on the pilot corpus with a stated projection to Corpus scale (storage in the Tribunal frankensqlite store); (d) a replay rig prototype diffs a hand-written toy unifier against traces to prove the comparison machinery. Traces are fixtures, never components; the generator lives behind the D8 wall. Tests: the pilot traces + replay rig land in fln-conformance as the seed of the continuous golden-trace lanes.

title: G0-10: Dependency-closure audit + authoritative SUITE.lock
description:
G0-10 Dependency-closure audit + SUITE.lock (§22.1-10, D1, §2.10): generate the FULL transitive dependency closure of the workspace (runtime, build, dev, fuzz tracked under separate policies), allowlist every package (name/version/source/checksum, features, license, build-script and proc-macro status, native-link status, unsafe-audit status, reason, owner, upgrade policy), commit SUITE.lock (exact commits/checksums for every FrankenSuite repo, the rustc nightly, target-feature sets, the Reference pin v4.32.0 with its commit hash, and the Corpus pin — one ceremony), and stand up the extraction scripts for the ABI/olean/extern contracts in CI.

The doctrine being enforced: authoritative fln-* crates introduce NO new unreviewed direct runtime dependencies; the audit is an owned fln-conformance check; CI fails on any unlisted package; no serde, no tokio, no rocksdb, no LLVM, no cranelift, no gmp-sys, no external SAT solver. Also prices risk R16 (suite-dependency drift — an unapproved transitive dep inside a reused suite crate is a finding, not a shrug).

Acceptance: (a) the closure inventory generated mechanically and checked in; (b) allowlist review complete with zero unlisted packages and zero policy violations; (c) SUITE.lock committed and CI builds ONLY from the lock; (d) contract-extraction jobs wired into CI failing on drift among pin, contract docs, and generated constants modules. Tests: the audit runs on every CI build forever; an allowlist diff requires an explicit review artifact.

title: W1: fln-core — names, levels, Expr inventory, options, positions
description:
Implement fln-core (§21, §1.1): the shared vocabulary every crate compiles against. Names (hierarchical, with macro-scope decoration hooks for Quill), universe levels (zero/succ/max/imax/param/mvar with the normalization laws incl. imax u 0 = 0 — Prop impredicativity depends on it), the kernel expression inventory (bvar de Bruijn, fvar, mvar, sort, const+levels, app, lam, forallE, letE, lit Nat/String, mdata, proj) with the Reference-observable accessories: cached hashes, loose-bvar ranges, has-fvar/has-mvar flags (metaprograms and fast traversal pruning read these — they are API, not internals). Options model with the canonical resource limits (maxHeartbeats 200000 in thousand-units, maxRecDepth 512) and the newer resource-limit surface. Byte positions in UTF-8 exactly as upstream computes them (wire-visible through diagnostics and LSP).

Distinct newtypes per semantic kind from day one (DeclNameId, ExprNodeId, BinderDepth, ReductionFuel, EnvCommitId — §8.2b): no integer is accepted as two semantic kinds anywhere in the workspace.

Acceptance: constructor inventory + flag semantics verified against C0 micro-fixtures derived from the pin; hashing observables match the Reference on the fixture corpus. Tests: property tests for level normalization laws; flag-computation unit tests; fixture diff harness wired into fln-conformance.

title: W1: fln-hash — owned hashing, domain separation, canonical serialization
description:
Implement fln-hash (§21): owned BLAKE3-class hashing with a domain-separation registry and canonical serialization schemas — the identity layer beneath the Ledger (decl hashes, logical roots), Grimoire (semantic hash vs byte hash, §7.3), receipts, the transparency log, and every cache key in the program.

Requirements: (a) domain separation is a REGISTRY, not a convention — every hash use site declares its domain tag and CI greps for unregistered hashing; (b) canonical serialization schemas are versioned, with the schema registry shared with the codecs (Appendix B: every durable format specified once, generated into both the codecs and the conformance corpus); (c) the semantic-hash / byte-hash distinction is structural: re-encoding and compression can change bytes without pretending to change meaning; (d) logical roots (§7.1) digest declarations + extension deltas + options while EXCLUDING wall-clock, paths, and scheduler traces — two hosts producing the same trusted environment share a logical root.

Acceptance: hash vectors checked in and stable across platforms; nothing in the program hashes outside this crate (CI-enforced). Tests: unit tests per hash domain with frozen vectors (cross-platform identity on the certified matrix); canonical-serialization round-trip property tests over generated values; a domain-confusion mutation test (same bytes hashed under two domains MUST differ, and a planted unregistered hash call must fail CI); logical-root determinism e2e test — two elaboration runs on different thread counts and (in CI) different hosts produce identical roots, with the full input closure logged in the test output for diagnosis on mismatch.

title: W1: Contract extraction — ABI/OLEAN contracts, extern + builtin censuses (generated, never transcribed)
description:
Build the contract-extraction toolchain (Rule D5/D9, Appendix B, Appendix C): checked-in scripts that parse the PINNED Reference sources and emit (a) ABI_CONTRACT.md + a Rust constants module — object header field offsets/widths, tag values per category, size-class and scalar-packing rules, the full exported lean_* function census with signatures and per-parameter ownership conventions, and the per-symbol status taxonomy (NativeSafe / RawPlatform / CompatWrapper / ReferenceSemanticAdapter / Unsupported — no unclassified symbol); (b) OLEAN_CONTRACT.md — header fields, versioning, relocation-table format, ModuleData field inventory, extension-entry encoding, plus the .ilean field contract; (c) the extern census (@[extern] symbols in Init/Std/Lean) feeding the Golem intrinsic table; (d) the builtin census with the Mirror partition class per symbol (toolchain-api / library-code / user-facing-data).

The law: layout constants are DERIVED, never remembered. CI re-runs extraction against the pin on every contract-job run and fails on drift among pin, contract docs, and generated constants. The census diff is the first artifact of every future epoch advance (§22.5).

Acceptance: all four extractions run mechanically from a cold checkout of vendor/lean4-src; generated constants compile; G0-1 consumed them successfully; CI drift job green. Tests: extraction determinism (two runs byte-identical); a seeded-drift mutation (perturb one constant, CI must fail).

title: W1: Tribunal bootstrap — epoch lab, differential harness, Parity Ledger schema
description:
Bootstrap the Tribunal (fln-conformance, §18) FIRST — before the subsystems it will judge — so every workstream is born differential. The Reference runs INSIDE the harness as the standing differential oracle, forever (Oracle-Only Law capacity 1).

Scope: the epoch laboratory (official release archives with hashes; executable/symbol/header inventories; CLI and LSP transcripts; artifact fixtures; quirk/defect ledgers; environment-normalization recipes; exact reproduction commands; per platform; immutable once published — discoveries create reviewed hashed revisions); the oracle-precedence ladder implemented as data (core theory > observed binary behavior > sources/tests > stable downstream dependence > documented divergence > unclassified-blocks); comparison classes as versioned normalizer code with fixtures (a normalizer may strip only declared-nonsemantic fields and can never discard an error body to pass); the corpus family scaffolding C0-C9; upstream test-suite import at the pin with expected-outcome mapping; the Parity Ledger schema (row-per-symbol: surface, symbol, kind, semantic status, L-level, mode, oracle kind, comparison class, fixtures, determinism class, claim state, freshness) with the regeneration + regression CI job; the ORACLE_FALLBACK poison-tag machinery and the release compile-out check (§18.10, D8).

Acceptance: a Reference-vs-Reference smoke differential runs green end-to-end (proves harness plumbing); the Parity Ledger generates and the dashboard aggregates; C1 corpus imported. Tests: the harness tests itself via seeded-divergence fixtures (a planted diff must be detected and triaged, never normalized away).

title: W1: Baseline performance report — measure the Reference before ratifying gates
description:
Produce the W1 baseline performance report (§19.1): measure the Reference at the pin on the two pinned bare-metal profiles (32-core x86-64 Linux; Apple-silicon macOS) BEFORE any gate number is finalized. Every §19.2 gate ratio is ratified or amended against this report — a gate number without a measured baseline is marketing.

Measure: full-Corpus build wall (cold and warm-cache); per-file elaboration time distribution (p50/p95/p99); kernel-recheck time of the olean set; server worker import/attach time and RSS per worker on representative mathlib files; first-goal latency distribution; the interpreter micro-corpus; the bv_decide corpus with CaDiCaL. Record host facts, isolation checks, cache states, sample counts, and RAW samples (runs failing host-quality checks are retained as invalid, never averaged in — §19.2 discipline).

Acceptance: the report is a checked-in, regenerable artifact (script + data + rendered summary) with every number carrying corpus, machine, cache state, and dispersion; the PG-1..PG-M table is re-ratified against it and any amendment is recorded in the plan decision log. Tests: the measurement scripts are deterministic in methodology (seeded ordering, pinned thread counts) and re-runnable by one command; a second run on the same host must reproduce medians within the declared variance budget.

title: W1: Windows functional CI — early platform honesty, certification deferred
description:
Stand up windows-x86-64 functional CI from W1 (§18.3, D17): windows is FUNCTIONAL from the start (build, unit tests, the growing conformance subset), while bit-certification under --reproducible remains a separate declared decision made later (the manim precedent).

Why early: platform assumptions fossilize fast — path handling, UTF-16 position conversions at the LSP boundary, dlopen vs LoadLibrary in the plugin door, mmap semantics in region loading, process spawning in the D2 subprocess protocol. Discovering these at W12 would mean re-opening frozen interfaces. The certified matrix (linux-x86-64, linux-aarch64, macos-aarch64) runs the determinism closure; windows runs the functional lanes and reports honestly in the Parity Ledger platform column.

Acceptance: CI matrix includes windows building the workspace and running the unit + conformance-smoke lanes; platform-conditional code carries explicit capability errors (never silent substitution); the Parity Ledger platform column reports windows rows at their honest L-levels. Tests: the CI lane itself, plus a fixture asserting that platform-gated features fail closed with typed CapabilityDenied errors rather than degrading silently.

title: W2: Marrow object model + tri-state RC — the lean_object ABI twin
description:
Implement the Marrow object model + tri-state reference counting (§6.1, §6.1b, §6.2; crates fln-rt + fln-unsafe-abi): the Reference runtime object model, exactly, under the exported lean_* names — object header (RC word, memory-kind/other byte, tag byte), constructor objects with scalar-field packing, arrays/scalar arrays/strings (UTF-8 with byte-length/capacity semantics), closures (arity, fixed args), thunks, tasks, IO.Refs, external objects with class vtables, bignum objects (fln-bignum-backed mpz-shim view), small Nats as odd tagged pointers ((n << 1) | 1). Every layout constant comes from the W1 generated contract tables.

Two heaps, one membrane: CompatHeap (exact ABI, membrane-only allocation, all unsafe confined to fln-unsafe-abi with ledger rows per site) vs NativeHeap (typed asupersync region handles); generated converters with declared ownership and failure semantics; lazy boundary-specific conversion (a metaprogram that never inspects a term never forces its Compat projection — memory-doubling risk R10 is the named gate PG-6, not an accident). Tri-state RC: single-threaded fast path, atomic shared, persistent-never-counted — matching upstream so ABI-crossing borrow/inc/dec behaves identically. Hardened builds: debug ownership shadows (double-release + foreign-pointer misuse detection, quarantine, deterministic replay events).

Structural laws (D3): no fln-unsafe-* crate depends on fln-kernel/fln-checker; no unsafe export launderable into a checked declaration; panic law at the ABI boundary (§6.5). Acceptance: G0-1/G0-3 findings folded; C4 probes pass both directions on the implemented subset. Tests: layout unit tests generated FROM the contract module; RC balance property tests; ownership-shadow mutation tests.

title: W2: Owned allocator — size-classed, heartbeat hook, membrane discipline
description:
Implement the owned allocator (§6.3): size-classed, mimalloc-tradition (thread-local pages, size-class bins, deferred frees) — owned rather than off-the-shelf because allocation behavior is SEMANTICALLY visible through heartbeat accounting (fuel parity, §4.2/§8.5) and performance-visible everywhere.

The heartbeat hook lives here: one counting law, calibrated by spike G0-6, shared by both kernel engines and Golem (allocation through Marrow so heartbeats count identically across interpreted and native paths). The allocator must also serve the CompatHeap discipline (§6.1b): membrane-only allocation and free, so foreign code observes exactly the Reference allocation contract at the ABI.

Acceptance: fuel-parity fixtures from G0-6 stay green when running over the real allocator; allocation-heavy corpus benchmarks show no pathological regression vs the baseline report envelope; zero leaks over the PG-6 four-hour soak profile (measured under the lab runtime with the leak oracle). Tests: size-class boundary unit tests; concurrent alloc/free stress under the asupersync lab with seed replay; heartbeat-count determinism test at thread counts {1, 8, 32} (same input, same tick totals — FL-INV-01 applies to fuel too).

title: W2: Compacted regions — construction, mmap load, relocation, page sharing
description:
Implement compacted regions (§6.4): region construction (object-graph serialization with pointer relocation tables), region loading (mmap, relocate-or-copy fallback, page-sharing across consumers — the enabler of the Lantern shared import heap §7.5), and region hygiene (immutability enforcement, persistent-RC marking).

Design law: this is deliberately THE SAME code path the Grimoire olean codec uses — olean fidelity and runtime-region fidelity are one test surface, so every codec round-trip test also exercises the runtime region machinery and vice versa. mmap and arena primitives beyond what asupersync region heaps provide live in fln-unsafe-region with ledger rows (D3).

Acceptance: a real mathlib olean region loads via mmap with relocation and is traversable as live objects (G0-1 promotion to production code); page-sharing verified across two consumers of one loaded region (RSS accounting shows shared pages, the PG-4/PG-6 mechanism); immutability violations trap in hardened builds. Tests: relocate-or-copy fallback forced and verified; corrupted-relocation-table fuzzing under resource budgets (hostile olean is a DoS surface even in safe Rust — R18); crash-during-construction drills leaving no half-published region (atomic staging).

title: W2: Grimoire environment — persistent maps, O(1) snapshots, extension registry, logical roots
description:
Implement the Grimoire environment (fln-env, §7.1): semantically the Reference environment (name -> constant map over axioms/defs/theorems/opaques/inductives/ctors/recursors/quotients; environment extensions with import-time replay preserving upstream ordering semantics exactly; attribute state; import graph), mechanically ours: persistent HAMT-style maps with structural sharing so snapshots are O(1) — the primitive under Athanor speculative parallelism (§10.6), Lantern per-request views (§14.3), and Envoy search trees (§16.3).

Key contracts: mathlib custom extensions round-trip as region data WITHOUT executing code; an extension payload understood only as opaque bytes is preserved losslessly, flagged in provenance, and honestly blocks fine-grained invalidation through it (never guessed safe); every extension declares merge + checkpoint semantics in a registry (branch/merge includes extensions BY CONTRACT); each commit carries a logical root (digest excluding wall-clock/paths/schedule — the cache key the Ledger, receipts, and Envoy all speak) plus a separate operational-metadata root.

Acceptance: environments decoded from real mathlib oleans (G0-1) materialize with extension replay matching Reference-observable order; snapshot fork + read costs O(1) verified; logical roots stable across hosts for identical inputs. Tests: extension-ordering differential fixtures; snapshot isolation property tests (mutation after fork invisible to the fork); logical-root determinism across thread counts and two machines.

title: W2: Term store — total interning, alpha-canonical digest, expanded-weight budgets
description:
Implement the term store with total contractual interning (§7.1b): expressions, levels, and names hash-consed contractually, not opportunistically — one node per full structural identity INCLUDING binder names and mdata (metaprograms observe them; faithful Expr behavior demands exact round-trip), with pointer/ID equality BEING structural equality: Expr.equal collapses to a compare; kernel caches key on identity pairs.

Plus: an auxiliary alpha-canonical digest (binder names stripped) indexing kernel-side defeq/whnf caches so alpha-varied duplicates share reduction work without confusing the metaprogram-visible plane. Sharing is maximal and MEASURED: every store keeps sharing statistics and enforces expanded-weight budgets — a term small on the wire can be astronomical unshared; an adversarial DAG-bomb is a first-class typed inspectable condition (§8.2c counts expanded weight, not bytes). Serialization preserves sharing exactly in both codecs.

Acceptance: interning invariant (structural equal implies pointer equal) holds under property tests across construction paths; alpha-digest cache sharing demonstrated on the G0-7 defeq fixtures; DAG-bomb fixtures produce typed Inconclusive under budget, never OOM or hang. Tests: sharing-statistics assertions on Corpus-derived fixtures; round-trip sharing preservation through both codecs; concurrent interning determinism at {1,8,32} threads.

title: W2: olean codec read — three validation levels over the extracted contract
description:
Implement the olean codec, read side (fln-olean, §7.2): header parsing (magic, version fields, base address), region relocation via the shared compacted-region machinery, ModuleData traversal into Grimoire environment structures, and the three validation levels — trust-the-producer (mmap speed, the Reference posture), structural audit (tag/bounds/reachability sweep, still fast), full recheck (Crucible over every constant — the G1 mode).

The layout inventory comes from OLEAN_CONTRACT.md (W1 extraction) with per-field provenance and a versioning note per Reference release in the supported epoch set. Hostile inputs are a first-class threat (R18): every decode path runs under resource budgets; a malformed olean yields typed ArtifactCorrupt / ArtifactEpochMismatch, never a panic and never unbounded work.

Acceptance: the full mathlib olean set at the pin loads at every validation level; read throughput meets the PG-8 floor (>= Reference load throughput) on the baseline profile; epoch-mismatch inputs produce the typed error with the correct epoch fields. Tests: the C3 artifact-archaeology corpus (valid + mutated oleans, every offset and tag targeted); decode-budget exhaustion fixtures; differential load (same olean into Reference and FrankenLean, environment statement-diff empty).

title: W2: olean codec write + .ilean — byte-identical faithful emission, atomic promotion
description:
Implement the olean codec, write side + .ilean (§7.3, §7.3b): region construction the Reference loads — identical header discipline, identical object layout — and in faithful mode BYTE-IDENTICAL output for FL-INV-04 (read a Reference olean, rebuild, diff bytes = empty; then the harder fresh-elaboration direction for the deterministic subset, residual freedoms pinned by registered CGSE policies per spike G0-5).

Two identities travel with every stored object: semantic hash (canonical meaning under the schema) and byte hash (stored encoding). Cross-file artifact sets (.olean + .ilean + native siblings) promote through a transaction manifest: staged, verified, then published atomically together or rolled back together — a crash mid-promotion leaves unreachable staging, never a half-published module. The .ilean codec is an exact epoch projection (spans, identifier occurrences, resolution targets with the pin field order, omissions, and position conventions) generated from the same normative schema layer as the olean codec so writer and checker cannot skew; richer server metadata lives in Palimpsest — .ilean stays exactly as boring as the ecosystem expects.

Acceptance: FL-INV-04 round-trip green at 100% of the Corpus set (PG-8); Reference toolchain loads and checks our written oleans (mixed-producer matrix seed); kill -9 drills at every promotion step leave recoverable state. Tests: byte-diff rigs per corpus file; .ilean field-order fixtures; the fault drill family (§18.2) for promotion.

title: W2: Effects on asupersync — IO/Task semantics, lab-virtualizable, capability-observed
description:
Implement effects on asupersync (§6.7): IO, timers, filesystem, process, and task pools behind the Reference IO/Task semantics — Task.spawn/bind/get observable ordering, IO.Ref atomicity, IO.Process behavior, the interruption/checkSystem polling contract — displacing the vendored-libuv event loop wholesale.

Under the lab runtime the ENTIRE effect plane virtualizes (virtual time, injected faults, seeded schedules): this is how the Tribunal replays server sessions and torture-tests cancellation, and it is why no detached thread or raw OS handle may bypass the Cx discipline anywhere in the workspace. In faithful mode default ambient authority approximates an ordinary Lean process (drop-in means unchanged packages just run, with effects OBSERVED into provenance); sound mode can require declared capabilities and fail closed; the --pure-elab audit mode (W5) builds on this seam.

Acceptance: the G0-3 IO/Task parity micro-corpus passes on the production effect plane, then again under the lab runtime with virtual time (same observable results — the virtualization is invisible to compliant code); cancellation drains regions deterministically (obligation-leak oracle clean). Tests: Task ordering fixtures incl. nested spawn/bind chains; IO.Ref atomicity stress under lab schedules; checkSystem interruption-latency fixtures; capability-observation events verified in the provenance journal.

title: W2: fln-libm — owned deterministic transcendentals, bit-locked across platforms
description:
Implement fln-libm (§6.8): owned, fixed-implementation Float transcendentals — sin, cos, tan, exp, log, pow, atan2, hypot (and the rest of the family the extern census enumerates) — with documented accuracy (<= 1 ulp target, per-function error tables published) and IDENTICAL BITS on every certified target, locked by cross-platform vectors in CI. No platform libm is linked, period (D21).

Why: Float arithmetic is hardware IEEE-754 binary64 (portable), but upstream defers transcendentals to platform libm, so compiled evaluation, #eval, and native_decide-class computation drift across glibc/macOS/Windows in the last ulps — the Reference cannot promise ITSELF cross-platform bit-stability (§1.6b). Kernel judgments never consume floats (Float ops are opaque to the kernel), so the soundness surface is untouched; the evaluation surface gains bit-stability recorded as a Behavior Note (platforms disagree with each other; we pick one correct answer and keep it everywhere).

Acceptance: cross-platform vector suite green on the certified matrix (identical bits linux-x86-64 / linux-aarch64 / macos-aarch64); error tables generated and published per function; a CI symbol-scan proves no libm import in any release binary. Tests: ulp-error measurement harness against high-precision references; boundary cases (subnormals, infinities, NaN payloads, huge args for range reduction); Float printing/parsing round-trip fixtures matching the Reference conventions.

title: W2: Inbound plugin door — dlopen Reference-built plugins, revocation-first, receipts
description:
Implement the inbound plugin door (§6.6): Reference-built shared libraries (precompileModules artifacts, user @[extern] libraries) dlopen into FrankenLean, lean_* imports resolved against Marrow exports, initializers run under Golem initialization ordering (§11.5).

Posture: a plugin is native code and is trusted as such; FrankenLean adds PROVENANCE (hash + origin in the build record), the --no-plugins audit mode (plugin-dependent declarations flagged), and revocation-first loading in sound mode (hash + trust-card check BEFORE dlopen, never after). Each load emits an initialization-DAG receipt so cycles, duplicate initializers, and late capability requests are explainable rather than opaque loader failures. The panic law holds at every exported boundary: no Rust panic crosses the ABI; wrappers catch, convert only what the epoch contract maps, and never fabricate a successful Lean result after an internal panic (§6.5).

Acceptance: real Reference-built mathlib-adjacent plugins load and run (G2 requirement, inbound half); --no-plugins elaboration flags exactly the dependent declarations; a deliberately crashing plugin isolates with a typed PluginCrashed error and a receipt, not process death in the daemon. Tests: the C4 cross-load matrix inbound lanes per platform; initializer-ordering fixtures; revocation-first denial fixtures (tampered hash never reaches dlopen).

design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — W2 owns the inbound loader membrane: generated-symbol resolution, revocation-before-load, trust-card/hash checks, provenance, panic isolation, allocator/RC correctness, --no-plugins dependency marking, and a typed initializer-handoff contract. Full Golem execution and Reference-observable initializer/extension ordering are not claimed until W5 initialization exists; franken_lean-golem-runtime-integration-pbe owns that no-mock integration. This bead now also depends on the owned allocator because a real plugin cannot honestly run on object-layout code alone.

title: W2: stage0 ABI gauntlet — upstream generated C runs the runtime suite against Marrow
description:
Stand up the stage0 ABI gauntlet as a standing Tribunal lane (§6.6, §18.2): compile the Reference own stage0-generated C (test apparatus only, via the optional D2 cc) against Marrow exported lean_* surface, and run the upstream runtime test suite through the membrane.

Why this is the strongest ABI rig available: stage0 is the exact code the ecosystem ships — millions of lines of generated C exercising every corner of the object model, RC discipline, boxing rules, and exported function census. If the membrane is wrong ANYWHERE, upstream own code says so, loudly and early, with a concrete failing call site. This displaces guesswork about ABI corner cases with an executable oracle. Note the D8 boundary: stage0 here is TEST APPARATUS (fixture corpus), never a runtime component; the lane runs in the Tribunal sandbox and its products are conformance evidence only.

Acceptance: the gauntlet builds and links against Marrow on linux-x86-64 first (other platforms staged); the upstream runtime suite passes on the implemented ABI subset with every failure triaged to a contract row (fix or honest Unsupported status); the lane runs in CI on membrane-touching changes. Tests: this IS the test; additionally a seeded-defect mutation (perturb one exported function ownership convention — the gauntlet must catch it, proving the lane kills mutants per §18.2).

title: W3: KERNEL_CONTRACT.md — the rule-by-rule Judgment Specification, written first
description:
Write KERNEL_CONTRACT.md — the Judgment Specification (§8.1b, Appendix A) — as the FIRST W3 deliverable, before any engine code: a rule-by-rule account of the implemented type theory, each rule anchored to (a) the Reference source lines that define it and (b) the literature that justifies it. Both kernel engines, the independent checker, and the Tribunal term generators are built against this document.

Inventory (Appendix A): typing rules for every expression constructor; universe-level normalization incl. imax _ 0 = 0; defeq — beta, eta (functions AND structures), delta ordered by definitional height with reducibility-independence, zeta, iota incl. K-like subsingleton elimination, definitional proof irrelevance in Prop, Quot.lift/Quot.mk computation, Nat/String literal rules with the exact accelerated Nat operation set (add, sub, mul, div, mod, pow, gcd, beq, ble, blt, land/lor/lxor/shifts as the pin defines); inductive validation (strict positivity, universe constraints, large elimination, mutual, nested-to-mutual, reflexive); structure eta/projection coherence; declaration admission rules; partial/unsafe quarantine; environment uniqueness rules; resource accounting hooks (counted, never semantic — exhaustion is a verdict about a run, never about a term, FL-INV-07).

The inversion that is itself a product: the Reference specification is its C++; FrankenLean specification is a document the C++ is CHECKED AGAINST, rule by rule, by the differential harness. Acceptance: every rule row has a Reference source anchor, fixtures listed or stubbed, and an unambiguous statement a second implementer could code from; review pass against G0-2 findings; versioned per epoch. Tests (the document is CI-checked like code): an anchor-resolution check verifies every cited Reference source location exists at the pin and fails on drift; a coverage check cross-references the rule inventory against the C0 fixture families (every rule names at least one fixture or an explicit stub with an owner); the Parity Ledger kernel rows link back to rule ids and CI fails on a dangling link; spec-change discipline — any edit to a rule statement requires touching its linked fixtures in the same change or CI flags the divergence, with the diff logged rule-by-rule.

title: W3: Crucible K1 — the certified small-step evaluator under the covenant
description:
Implement Crucible engine K1, the certified evaluator (§8.3): a direct, boring, small-step-flavored implementation of the full judgment inventory, optimized for AUDITABILITY — the code a skeptical logician reads in an afternoon. K1 is the definitional reference for K2, the engine forced by --kernel k1 for audit builds, and the engine compiled into the WASM Judge.

Constraints (the covenant, §8.1): fln-kernel <= 12 KLOC total across both engines, forbid(unsafe_code), dependencies only fln-bignum + Grimoire read-only NativeHeap term views + core/alloc; zero I/O, zero threads, zero global mutable state, zero plugin hooks; one exported authority: check(Environment, Declaration) -> Verdict. The covenant is CI-enforced: line count + dependency-graph walk + TCB inventory generated from the actual graph every run; growth requires amending the plan first. Typestate admission (§8.2b) and the typed resource algebra (§8.2c) are part of this bead: budgets from the caller Cx (node count, depth, expanded weight, reduction steps, allocation), cooperative cancellation checkpoints, no recursive host traversal over attacker-controlled depth, exhaustion = typed Inconclusive with a consumption profile.

Acceptance: G0-2 module set re-checked by production K1 with verdicts identical to lean4checker; every KERNEL_CONTRACT rule maps to named code + fixtures; DAG-bomb and deep-term fixtures produce Inconclusive under budget. Tests: rule-per-rule C0 micro-fixtures; generated well-typed term storms (agreement with the Reference kernel); mutation campaign — a skipped positivity check or inverted universe condition must be killed by a named test (§18.2).

title: W3: Crucible K2 — NbE accelerator, memoized whnf, cross-checked against K1
description:
Implement Crucible engine K2, the NbE accelerator (§8.3): normalization-by-evaluation with environment-aware closures, memoized whnf keyed on (term-hash, transparency), lazy delta with definitional-height comparison — the engine that makes decide, big Fin computations, and mathlib defeq-heavy corners fast (the PG-1/PG-2 arithmetic depends on it; the design was priced by spike G0-7).

Laws: K2 implements the SAME judgments as K1 (one KERNEL_CONTRACT); K2 verdicts are spot-audited against K1 continuously (sampled per run, exhaustively in Tribunal soaks) and any disagreement fails loudly and ships as a fixture; every fast-lane shortcut carries exactly one registered justification — a local proof argument in the kernel design note, a certificate checked by a simpler routine, or differential evidence (which licenses an optimization and never impersonates a proof). The size covenant counts K2 lines too. Cache keying uses the alpha-canonical digest lane (§7.1b) so alpha-varied duplicates share reduction work invisibly to metaprograms.

Acceptance: the G0-7 twenty-problem tail suite meets the envelope the PG-2 model requires; K1/K2 continuous cross-check wired and green on the full fixture corpus; PG-1 trajectory measured (full mathlib olean recheck time on the 32-core profile). Tests: differential K1-vs-K2 on generated term storms; memoization-correctness property tests (cached verdict == fresh verdict under transparency changes); fuel-parity fixtures shared with G0-6 stay green on both engines.

title: W3: fln-checker — structurally independent checker, WASM-clean, consensus seat
description:
Implement fln-checker, the independent kernel checker (§8.3b): a deliberately DIFFERENT second implementation, not a feature flag over fln-kernel — separate normalization algorithm (eager where K1/K2 are lazy), simpler persistent maps and substitutions, its OWN decoder over Grimoire canonical wire objects (never the primary heap nodes), no dependency on the unsafe membrane, an independently derived error-class mapping, a separate build artifact, and a WASM build for a foreign execution substrate (the WASM Judge second engine).

Non-sharing is proven STRUCTURALLY: CI verifies fln-checker shares data schemas but no semantic implementation modules with fln-kernel (dependency-graph rules). The checker joins the consensus policies of §8.3c: standard (deterministic content-seeded sample + all high-risk forms — never runtime randomness), release (full closure), paranoid (everywhere, optionally distinct hosts). The honest economics: roughly 2x kernel CPU where it runs, single-digit percent end-to-end (PG-1b measures it). Common-mode risk R6 (shared Judgment Specification misreading) is why FOREIGN witnesses (lean4checker, lean4lean via export) exist in the council; this bead includes wiring the external-witness sampling seam.

Acceptance: full agreement with K1/K2 on the corpus; a seeded spec-divergence mutant (deliberately misimplement one rule in a test build) is caught by the differential lane; WASM build passes the same vectors bit-for-bit. Tests: non-sharing CI check; consensus-policy integration tests where a planted disagreement HALTS publication with both traces attached (never outvoted).

title: W3: fln-bignum — owned kernel-grade bignum, ABI-layout limbs, PG-K gate
description:
Implement fln-bignum (§8.4): kernel-grade arbitrary precision replacing GMP — limb-based magnitude representation matching the ABI bignum-object layout (Marrow mpz-shim is a VIEW, not a conversion), schoolbook/Karatsuba/Toom multiplication with thresholds tuned on the KERNEL-REDUCTION corpus (not synthetic benchmarks), Knuth-D division, binary/Lehmer gcd, exact decimal parsing/printing for literals. Constant-time-ness explicitly NOT claimed (this is a prover, not a KMS).

Why owned: GMP sits under every literal in the Reference and inside kernel reduction itself (§1.10) — an unauditable C dependency in the most trusted position. The kernel accelerated Nat operation set (add/sub/mul/div/mod/pow/gcd/comparisons/bitwise per the pin) routes through fln-bignum so decide-class proofs keep the same asymptotics. The crate stays in-repo rather than the suite (kernel-adjacent code answers to the kernel covenant; revisit trigger recorded if a second suite member needs bigints — §2.11).

Acceptance: PG-K gate — kernel-reduction operation mix <= 1.15x the GMP-backed Reference kernel on the arithmetic-heavy fixture set (parity on the small operands that dominate; bounded, PUBLISHED gap on giants); ABI-layout view verified against C4 probes. Tests: differential vectors vs known-good results across the full operation set incl. boundary limb counts; parsing/printing round-trips vs Reference outputs; threshold-tuning harness checked in with the corpus profile it tuned against.

title: W3: Certificates, receipts, transparency log — auditable checking
description:
Implement certificates, receipts, and the transparency log (§8.6): every checked declaration can emit a certificate — the compressed, structure-shared elaborated term DAG plus reduction hints that make independent rechecking cheap (unfold order, literal-arithmetic transcripts so a tiny verifier replays accelerated ops without its own bignum trust). Certificates attach to olean-next records, feed fln export (speaking the community established export format for cross-prover checkers), and may ship a warm defeq-cache cartridge (memoized reduction history — third-party rechecking becomes verification rather than search).

The certificate law (D20): the checker must be SIMPLER than recomputation, must reject unknown versions, and must fall back to recomputation on any failure — an accelerator, never a wider TCB. Receipts bind kernel + checker build hashes, the consensus policy, fuel profiles, and both verdicts. Attested receipts append to a transparency log (Merkle Mountain Range over receipt roots, signed checkpoints): third parties verify inclusion and consistency without trusting the logger; equivocation is detectable — "checked" becomes an artifact anyone can audit, not a verb someone witnessed.

Acceptance: certificate emit + independent replay on the corpus with the checker measurably simpler (line count, dependency count) than the engines; fln export output accepted by at least one external checker; MMR inclusion/consistency proofs verified by an independent implementation of the verifier side; PG-1b receipt-emission overhead <= 1 ms/decl amortized. Tests: certificate-version rejection fixtures; corrupt-certificate fallback-to-recompute fixtures; log equivocation detection test (forked checkpoint must be caught).

title: W3: fln check-olean — the Independent Judge CLI and the G1 evidence bundle
description:
Ship fln check-olean — the Independent Judge (§8.6, G1): the standalone auditor that re-checks an entire olean set (all of mathlib in minutes) on a kernel that shares no code, no compiler, and no bignum with the thing it is checking — the program FIRST public artifact, shipped before FrankenLean has elaborated a single file.

Composition: Grimoire read (full-recheck validation level) + Crucible K1/K2 + fln-checker per consensus policy + receipts + optional certificate emission. CLI contract: stable exit codes, --json output (versioned schema), per-declaration verdicts with stable error classes, a summary receipt binding the input set hash, engine build hashes, policy, and totals; --receipts writes the receipt set + transparency-log append. Performance: PG-1 — full mathlib olean set <= 5 min on the 32-core profile, <= 25 min single-thread.

This bead includes the G1 gate evidence assembly: zero acceptance divergence against the Reference toolchain across the full mathlib olean set at the pin, every divergence (if any) triaged under the §8.2 oracle-or-spec-investigation-first law. Acceptance: G1 declared with the evidence bundle (run logs, receipts, Parity Ledger rows, PG-1 numbers on the baseline machine). Tests: CLI transcript fixtures (exit codes, --json schema); interrupted-run drills (partial output never masquerades as complete); determinism — two runs produce identical receipts.

title: W3: Kernel differential rigs — foreign witnesses, term storms, delta-debugging
description:
Stand up the standing kernel differential rigs (§8.7, §18.2): Crucible vs the Reference kernel (via lean4checker on shared oleans); Crucible vs lean4lean verified rules (fixture translation through the export format); K1 vs K2 continuous; fuzzed-term storms (well-typed generators + mutation) where ANY pairwise disagreement is a finding — the kernel is the one subsystem where we volunteer to be outnumbered.

Triage law (§8.2, R7): a Reference-accepted / Crucible-rejected finding opens an oracle-or-spec INVESTIGATION first — never auto-classified as our defect or theirs; if the Reference accepts a term the type theory rejects (upstream soundness bug — it has happened), FrankenLean matches the THEORY, files the reproducer upstream, and records the Behavior Note (soundness beats bug-parity, D23). Any soundness-side divergence in our direction is a stop-ship publish-the-finding event. Every finding auto-shrinks via the Tribunal delta-debugger into a minimal fixture.

Acceptance: all four rig families running in CI on the fixture corpus + nightly on larger storms; external-witness sampling (lean4checker/lean4lean seats in the §8.3c council on sampled classes) wired; the delta-debugger produces minimal reproducers automatically. Tests: seeded-divergence mutants per rule family must be detected by the rigs (mutation-kill evidence per §18.2); rig determinism (same seed, same storm, same verdicts).
