title: W4: Quill engine — Pratt parser, lossless green trees, exact SourceInfo
description:
Implement the Quill engine (fln-parse, fln-syntax; §9.1): token tables + Pratt-precedence parsing with the Reference category/leading/trailing model; Syntax trees with identical node kinds, atom/ident/node structure, and SourceInfo discipline (original vs synthetic positions, leading/trailing whitespace attachment, UTF-8 byte positions exactly as upstream computes them — wire-visible, therefore conformance surface); the full extensibility contract: parsing INTERLEAVES with elaboration (a command can install parsers used three lines later), commands install syntax/macro/elab rules visible immediately, precedence interactions match.

Internals (ours to design): lossless rope + persistent green tree (exact bytes, trivia, comments, UTF-8/UTF-16 positions) with red views adding context pointers per document snapshot; every node carries an origin set (literal span, macro-definition span, macro-call span, quotation spans, recovered markers) projected to the Reference single-primary-span convention at the wire while the full provenance graph feeds Palimpsest.

Acceptance: G0-4 curated corpus trees/positions byte-agree; C1/C2 parse products diff clean against the Reference frontend under instrumentation; the interleaving contract demonstrated (fixture where a command installs syntax used later in the same file). Tests: token-table and precedence property tests; UTF-8/UTF-16 position conversion fixtures (LSP boundary); green-tree losslessness (parse then print = identical bytes) across the corpus.

title: W4: GrammarEpoch + memoization — grammar as versioned data, typed parser effects
description:
Implement GrammarEpoch versioning + parse memoization (§9.1): the grammar state as versioned DATA, not hidden globals — a GrammarEpoch content-hashes the active syntax declarations, parser descriptors, precedence tables, scoped notation, token parsers, and relevant options; every parse node binds the epoch it was recognized under; memoization keys on (position, category, precedence, GrammarEpoch); a syntax-changing command invalidates exactly the parse products that read the changed components.

Parser registrations emit typed effect summaries (AddsSyntax, AddsToken, ChangesPrecedence, OpensScope, RegistersMacro, OpaqueParserEffect) — an opaque effect is legal for fidelity but forces the conservative suffix barrier and disables distributed parsing past that point (precision is earned by evidence, never assumed; the system starts conservative and ratchets, §10.6).

Why load-bearing: this is what makes sub-command incremental reparse (Lantern), the Ledger notation/syntax invalidation-matrix row, and distributed parsing sound rather than hopeful. Acceptance: epoch hashing deterministic and stable across hosts; memoization hit/miss behavior verified against the invalidation matrix fixtures (a notation change invalidates exactly the affected epoch descendants after the activation point); opaque-effect barrier demonstrated. Tests: property tests — memoized parse == fresh parse under any epoch-respecting edit sequence; C8 incremental edit-sequence corpus with expected invalidation cones.

title: W4: Hygiene + macro transactions — stable generated names, memoizable expansion
description:
Implement hygiene + macro expansion transactions (§9.2): macro scopes exactly as the Reference implements them (scope-decorated names, Syntax quotation/antiquotation semantics, macro_rules matching), with generated identifiers derived from STABLE expansion paths (origin command -> macro invocation -> quotation path -> local ordinal) — never thread timing or allocator addresses, because metaprograms and diagnostics observe generated names (determinism of names is a compatibility requirement).

Macro expansion is a TRANSACTION: an invocation receives syntax + hygiene seed + grammar/extension snapshot + declared capabilities + a deterministic budget, and returns expanded syntax, generated names, messages, a source map, extension reads, and capability events. Memoizable exactly when all reads are known: a file-reading macro caches against the capability-recorded canonical path AND content hash; a clock-reading macro is opaque in faithful mode and denied by default in hardened sound profiles.

Acceptance: the G0-4 hygiene rig green on production code; expansion-path name derivation verified stable across thread counts and runs; transaction memoization correctness (cache hit == fresh expansion) under capability-read tracking. Tests: hygiene corner-case corpus (shadowing, splices, cross-macro capture); quotation/antiquotation round-trips; a capability-lying mutant (macro reads a file without declaring it) must be caught by the perturbation validator.

title: W4: Error recovery + speculative boundaries — repair without changing acceptance
description:
Implement error recovery + the speculative boundary map (§9.3): specified recovery — recovery productions per syntax category, resynchronization tokens, and the RULE that recovery may add diagnostics but never change acceptance. Two boundary products serve the editor: the exact command stream (Reference parser semantics, sole authority over meaning) and a speculative boundary map (a conservative scanner recovering enough structure after malformed edits to schedule work and show syntax diagnostics); speculative boundaries can never commit declarations, and disagreement between the two is journaled as a recovery event.

One law rules everything: no recovered node reaches Athanor without an explicit RecoveredSyntax marker, and no declaration dependent on an unacknowledged repair is ever published (Behavior Note BN-05: extra diagnostics after a syntax error are possible in sound mode; acceptance is unchanged; faithful mode reproduces upstream recovery behavior).

Plus sub-command incremental relex/reparse feeding Lantern snapshots (§14.3): edits inside a command re-lex and re-parse below command granularity where the GrammarEpoch permits. Acceptance: malformed-input corpus keeps serving diagnostics/completions downstream of errors; acceptance-invariance verified (recovery on/off changes no accept/reject verdict on the corpus); incremental reparse products identical to full reparse on the C8 edit sequences. Tests: fuzzed malformed inputs (grammar-mutation fuzzing) never panic and never change acceptance; recovery-event journaling fixtures; RecoveredSyntax-marker propagation tests.

title: W5: FIR pipeline — validated IR stages, pass witnesses, RC/FBIP disciplines
description:
Implement the FIR pipeline (fln-comp, §11.1): elaborated terms -> FIR (ANF/LCNF-class IR keeping the Reference-established compilation disciplines: specialization of instance/closed arguments, simplification, closure conversion, explicit boxing, reference-count insertion with borrow inference and constructor-reuse/FBIP analysis — kept because FBIP-style reuse is the performance model the ecosystem code is written against and is observable through performance cliffs) -> FLBC register bytecode.

Engineering law: every pipeline stage has a versioned IR schema, a deterministic printer, and a validator; every optimization pass is a pure ValidatedIr -> (ValidatedIr, PassWitness) transformation with declared preconditions, a preservation argument or executable validator, a resource-growth bound, deterministic ordering, and a fallback to its input. The RC-insertion pass runs an ownership validator (no use-after-consume, balanced joins and exception edges, capture correctness, ABI ownership contracts) deliberately SIMPLER than the optimizer it audits. Executable-closure selection records WHY each declaration is retained (direct call, closure capture, initializer, reflection, exported symbol, conservative opaque edge) so dead-code questions have answers.

Acceptance: the G0-3 micro-corpus compiles through the full pipeline with every pass witness validating; compiled results observably identical to interpretation of the unoptimized form (pass-preservation differential). Tests: IR printer/parser round-trips; ownership-validator mutation tests (a planted dropped-retain must be caught); pass-ordering determinism at {1,8,32} threads.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. title: W5: FLBC interpreter — ABI-valued registers, inline caches, PG-7 floor
description:
Implement the FLBC interpreter (fln-vm, §11.2): threaded dispatch, inline caches on constructor tags and closure arities, direct-call fast paths for the intrinsic table, allocation through Marrow (heartbeats count identically to native paths — fuel parity crosses the VM boundary), and the Reference semantics for panics, partial non-termination, stack depth, and checkSystem interruption polls.

The design masterstroke to preserve at all costs: FLBC values ARE Marrow ABI objects, so calls cross between interpreted code, Iron-compiled code, Reference-built plugins, and the Mirror native facade with ZERO conversion — one calling convention for the whole membrane (validated by spike G0-3). Performance floor PG-7: >= 3x the Reference interpreter on the tactic micro-corpus — deliberately conservative because the interpreter is the default vehicle for the long tail of user tactics (the interpreter tax is weakness census #5).

Acceptance: #eval/IO/Task parity corpus green (G2 requirement); PG-7 met on the micro-corpus against the W1 baseline; interruption polling honors the checkSystem contract (latency fixtures). Tests: opcode-level unit tests; VM opcode fuzzing under budgets (§18.2); inline-cache correctness under polymorphic call sites (cache hit == cache miss result); cross-boundary call matrix (interpreted <-> intrinsic <-> plugin) with RC-balance assertions.

Those arrive through fln-k63, franken_lean-hgu, and franken_lean-sno and are proven together by franken_lean-golem-runtime-integration-pbe.

title: W5: Intrinsic table — the extern census implemented natively, row-per-symbol evidence
description:
Implement the intrinsic table from the extern census (§11.4, Appendix C): every @[extern] symbol in Init/Std/Lean at the pin — Nat/Int/Array/ByteArray/String/Float ops, IO, Task, ST, environment access, compiler-API entry points — as native Rust intrinsics beneath the broader Mirror facade. The table is GENERATED from the census (mechanical extraction, W1), and the census diff is the first artifact of every epoch advance.

Semantics: Float arithmetic is IEEE-754 binary64 with the Reference printing/parsing conventions; transcendentals route through fln-libm (§6.8) — no platform libm on any path that can reach an artifact or receipt (D21); UInt*/USize follow the ABI width rules; @[implemented_by] swaps honored at the boundary the ecosystem respects. Every row carries fixtures and a Parity-Ledger L-level; unclassified rows do not exist (census totality).

Acceptance: the census fully populated with an implementation status per row (G2 requirement: fully populated with fixtures); differential parity on the intrinsic fixture corpus vs the Reference (bit class where applicable, obs class where documented); missing-intrinsic calls produce typed unsupported errors naming the row, never UB or a guess. Tests: per-row fixture families generated alongside the table; boundary-value sweeps for numeric ops; String UTF-8 position-convention fixtures; Float printing round-trips.

title: W5: Initialization + capability-scoped effects — ordering, --pure-elab, receipts
description:
Implement initialization + capability-scoped effects (§11.5): initialize / builtin_initialize ordering, import-time extension replay interleaving, and real-world IO effects exactly as upstream — under asupersync capabilities, so a --pure-elab audit mode can DENY ambient filesystem/network/clock to metaprograms and report exactly which declarations demanded them.

The posture: capability-scoped metaprogramming is a sound-mode observability win and a frontier-mode sandbox, NEVER a semantic change to compliant code (faithful mode default ambient authority approximates an ordinary Lean process — drop-in means unchanged packages just run, with effects OBSERVED into provenance). Initialization DAG receipts (from the plugin door, §6.6) unify with this: cycles, duplicate initializers, and late capability requests are explainable. This bead also covers the interaction with Golem-hosted lakefile execution (Ledger, §13.3) — lakefiles are user code with declared effect surfaces.

Acceptance: initializer-ordering fixtures match Reference-observable behavior incl. interleaving with extension replay; --pure-elab on a corpus with known effectful metaprograms reports exactly the offender set; capability events land in Palimpsest with correct spans. Tests: ordering property tests over generated init DAGs; denial fixtures (a denied capability yields typed CapabilityDenied naming the alternative, never silent substitution — D2 discipline); provenance-completeness checks against the perturbation validator.

title: W5: native_decide Tier 1 — replay receipts, trust-surface provenance
description:
Implement native_decide Tier 1 — replay receipts (§11.6): support native_decide with the Reference semantics and the Reference caveat stated LOUDER (it extends trust to Golem/Iron), plus the first stage of the honest repair: every reflection-class evaluation emits a replay receipt — function-entry digests over the registered reflection set, branch decisions, literal-arithmetic transcripts — recorded against term identities, replayable by Crucible at a stated cost multiple. Certified profiles verify always; standard samples by policy.

Trust bookkeeping shipped with it: provenance marks every native_decide-dependent theorem; fln audit --tcb lists a project trust surface (axioms, native_decide sites, plugins, facade classes); fln why-trusts <decl> walks the transitive trust cone (§15.6 seam — the Palimpsest query lands in W9, but the provenance emission lands here). Tier 2 (trace-guided kernel replay as certified-path default, G6) and Tier 3 (verified evaluator, research) are explicitly OUT of this bead — each tier stands alone and the trust ledger records which tier each build achieved, because "we no longer trust the compiler" is a claim with a scope.

Acceptance: receipts emitted for the reflection corpus; Crucible replay verifies them at the stated cost multiple; sampling policy configurable and journaled; the trust ledger row per build records the tier. Tests: receipt round-trip fixtures; a tampered-receipt fixture must fail verification; provenance-marking completeness on a corpus with known native_decide sites.

title: W6: Monadic tower + ElabTxn — explicit transactions, audited rollback, mvar graph
description:
Implement the monadic tower + ElabTxn (§10.1): the Core/Meta/TermElab/Tactic state SEMANTICS (metavariable contexts with delayed assignments, local contexts, backtracking discipline, option threading, message logs, InfoTree recording) as NativeHeap structures, with Mirror-facade views projected into Marrow objects exactly where user metaprograms touch them — the toolchain pays the ABI shape only at the seam it actually crosses (the Oracle-Only Law dividend, §6.1b).

Elaboration state is EXPLICIT, not ambient: each command runs in an ElabTxn (base logical root, local context, metavariable store, universe store, constraint queue, extension overlay, message buffer, InfoTree builder, capability context, decision ledger, deterministic seed, budget); nested elaboration uses child transactions with exactly five outcomes (commit all; commit diagnostics/info only; roll back completely; expose candidate + unresolved obligations; fork into deterministic alternatives). Rollback is AUDITED: Tribunal leak tests prove no failed alternative leaves assigned metavariables, registered declarations, or messages behind — the bug class upstream ambient monads make chronic is made structural here. Metavariables form an explicit dependency graph (type, context, kind, origin, assignment state + justification, delayed constraints) with targeted wake-up: assigning one reschedules only constraints that actually read it.

Acceptance: tower observable semantics verified against C1/C2 elaboration fixtures; leak tests green across the five outcomes; wake-up precision measured (no spurious rescheduling on the fixture set). Tests: transaction-outcome property tests; a planted leak mutant (child txn leaves an assignment behind) must be caught; InfoTree shape differentials vs the oracle.

title: W6: Unifier/defeq — the approximation ladder, implemented against golden traces
description:
Implement unification + defeq against golden traces (§10.2): transparency-aware whnf/isDefEq mirroring the Reference approximation ladder — syntactic fast paths, first-order approximation, Miller patterns, delayed assignments, postponement, unfolding by reducibility and definitional height, structure-eta awareness. Mathlib DEPENDS on the precise power of the unifier (proofs elaborate because a particular approximation fires); this is where bug-for-bug earns its keep and where Critical risk R2 lives.

Method (the R2 mitigation): implemented AGAINST the instrumented-oracle golden decision traces (G0-9 -> §18.3) — which approximation fired, what postponed, what unfolded, in what order — at Corpus scale, with trace-replay rigs running continuously. Every T3 divergence is triaged with the strong prior that it is a fidelity defect; a persistent divergence class becomes a faithful-mode blocker and a published erratum with quantified Corpus blast radius, never a quiet Behavior Note (the epoch ships R3, not R4, until closed). Failures are recorded as typed constraint cells (HasType, IsDefEq, SynthesizeInstance, Coerce, ResolveName, ElaboratePattern, ProveTermination) with origins, dependencies, attempts, and outcomes — the substrate Palimpsest blame slices are computed from.

Acceptance: trace-replay agreement on the pilot corpus, expanding toward Corpus scale with the divergence ledger empty or fully classified; the G0-7 tail suite green through the production engine; constraint-cell recording complete per the perturbation validator. Tests: golden-trace replay rigs per approximation stage; postponement-order fixtures; defeq differentials vs the oracle on C0-C2.

title: W6: Term elaboration — coercions, match compilation, recursion elaborators, exact generated names
description:
Implement term elaboration (§10.3): coercion insertion, implicit/strict-implicit/instance-implicit binder handling, auto-bound implicits, structure instances and anonymous constructors, dependent match compilation into auxiliary matchers with generated equation lemmas and splitters MATCHING the Reference generated-name and shape conventions (metaprograms pattern-match on these artifacts — they are API, not internals), deriving handler dispatch, attribute application, variable/section/namespace resolution with the Reference ambiguity rules, and the recursion elaborators (structural, well-founded with the same measure-synthesis surface, partial, unsafe) lowering to EXACTLY the kernel-visible constructions of §1.2 (recursors, WellFounded.fix, unfold equations — the kernel sees only these).

Termination elaboration records a machine-readable TerminationCard (call graph, candidate measures, generated obligations, chosen theorem dependencies) — the artifact the Bloodhound repair lane consumes when a termination proof breaks under migration (§16.1).

Acceptance: generated-name conventions byte-agree with the oracle on the match/equation-lemma corpus (C2 metaprogramming-weight packages); recursion lowerings kernel-check identically; TerminationCards emitted and schema-validated. Tests: match-compilation differentials incl. nested/overlapping patterns; equation-lemma shape fixtures; deriving-handler corpus; ambiguity-resolution fixtures per the Reference rules.

title: W6: Synod instance engine — exact semantics, SearchCards, keyed negative caches
description:
Implement Synod, the instance engine (§10.4): the Reference semantics — priorities, outParam/semiOutParam, default instances, tabling/caching behavior, the results the ecosystem diamond structures depend on — behind a rebuilt engine: instance search compiled to an indexed relational form (discrimination-automaton candidate retrieval, subsumption-checked tabling, cycle detection with franken_networkx diagnostics), with provenance recorded per resolution: every attempted instance, why it failed (unification residue, priority loss, depth), as a Palimpsest tree.

Every search emits a SearchCard (explored candidates, priorities, unification residues, cycle decisions, cache hits, selection) — provenance and negative-cache key in one artifact, with negative entries valid ONLY under their exact dependency key (instance-table identity, transparency, options, local-context fingerprint). Faithful mode pins search order to the Reference; sound mode may reorder only within proven-confluent regions (same result, better time), CGSE-registered. Name resolution, coercion graphs, and discrimination indexes are immutable content-addressed derivations of the environment commit, incrementally maintained and Ledger-cached — faithful iteration order preserved even where internals are faster.

Acceptance: instance-search outcomes match the oracle across the Corpus instance-trace corpus (instrumented-oracle instance traces); SearchCards complete per perturbation; the sound-mode "why is there no instance" answer (§15.3) renders from this engine exhaust with no second implementation. Tests: golden instance-trace replay; diamond/priority fixtures; negative-cache validity mutation tests (a stale negative entry must be impossible under the dependency key); cycle-diagnostic fixtures.

title: W6: Native tactic framework + bootstrap-safe primitive tactics behind facade rows
description:
Implement the native tactic framework + core tactic set (§10.5): goal management, metavariable assignment, evalTactic dispatch, elab_rules/macro_rules registration and matching, focusing/structuring combinators, evalExpr-class services, and the InfoTree hooks as Athanor code; the core tactic set (rfl-class closing, intro/apply/cases/induction machinery, simp via Anvil, decide via Crucible, omega/bv_decide via Verdict, norm_num-class evaluation) native behind Mirror facade rows — each with equivalence class, golden-trace fixtures, and its own Parity-Ledger L-level.

USER tactics — mathlib, aesop, project elab blocks — run on Golem and call this framework through the facade, receiving GoalState views and capability contexts. No tactic, native or user, can construct a trusted declaration except through Crucible (FL-INV-02). The framework observable semantics (state threading, backtracking, error propagation, InfoTree shape) are conformance surface, proven by the metaprogram-corpus rig running the ecosystem REAL tactic code.

Acceptance: the curated mathlib-tactic slice (G0-8) executes through the production framework with environments, InfoTrees, diagnostics, and generated names diffing clean against the oracle at declared L-levels; core tactics pass their per-row fixture families. Tests: tactic-framework state-threading property tests; backtracking/error-propagation fixtures; facade-row differential families per core tactic; FL-INV-02 mutation test (a tactic attempting direct environment admission must be structurally impossible — compile-time — or caught).

G3's curated metaprogram slice reports those levels explicitly; full engine-backed tactic functionality is preserved and completed before G4.

title: W6: Deterministic dataflow scheduler — speculative parallelism, canonical merge, perturbation-validated effects
description:
Implement the deterministic dataflow scheduler (Bet B4, §10.6): declarations as dataflow nodes — parse yields a conservative dependency pre-scan; elaboration proceeds speculatively in parallel asupersync regions against O(1) environment snapshots; a declaration that reads a not-yet-committed name suspends on its true dependency or aborts-and-retries; commits merge in CANONICAL ORDER (source order per module; module order per Ledger topology) so the resulting environment, diagnostics, and InfoTrees are those of sequential elaboration — bit-for-bit, at any thread count (FL-INV-01 by construction, the invariant upstream Elab.async demonstrably lacks).

Mechanics: optimistic concurrency — transactions get source-order IDs; commit-time read-set validation against the latest committed predecessor either commits in canonical order or discards/rebases; no last-writer-wins anywhere; conflicting extension mutations serialize. Side-effecting commands are ordering barriers detected conservatively: unknown effects serialize; known-commutative registered effects parallelize. Command effect summaries (ReadsDecl(name, Type|Value|Attributes), ReadsInstances(head), ReadsSimpSet, ReadsGrammar, ReadsOption, WritesDecl, WritesInstance, WritesGrammar, WritesEnvExtension, UsesCapability, Opaque(reason)) are captured dynamically and validated BY PERTURBATION: mutate an allegedly-unread input, require the product unchanged; a failing perturbation demotes the producer to Opaque and opens a Parity-Ledger bead. Cross-module speculation rides Ledger hashes-in-flight; mis-speculation costs a retry, never a wrong answer.

Acceptance: PG-5 thread-matrix {1,8,32} bit-identical environments/diagnostics/artifacts per commit on the corpus; speculation win measured against the sequential baseline; perturbation validator running in CI. Tests: DPOR-bounded schedule exploration of the merge protocol under the asupersync lab; abort/retry storm fixtures; seeded effect-summary lies must be demoted.

title: W6: Builtin census + Mirror facade — census-total, evidence-leveled, no hidden oracle
description:
Implement the builtin census + generated Mirror facade v1 (§4.3, Appendix C): mechanical extraction over the pinned Init/Std/Lean sources emitting the census (symbol, Lean signature, universe/binder telescope, module, kind, attributes, extern symbol, effect class, PARTITION CLASS: toolchain-api / library-code / user-facing-data) and, from the toolchain-api subset, generated facade stubs (upstream signature, telescope, effect class) whose bodies call the native Quill/Athanor/Crucible/Golem/Anvil/Marrow services.

The three laws: (1) census TOTALITY — no unclassified symbol; a symbol the census cannot classify blocks the surface L-level, never gets guessed. (2) Fidelity by evidence (FL-INV-03) — every toolchain-api row proven against the oracle: fixture differentials, golden decision traces, the metaprogram-corpus rig; a row whose API intrinsically exposes internal object identity or allocation order is classified at its honest L-level with a Behavior Note, never hand-waved. (3) No hidden oracle (D8) — the fallback for a facade defect is fixing the native implementation against fixtures; there is no run-the-upstream-definition switch in any release binary. Every row states: symbol, upstream semantics anchor (source location at the pin), equivalence class (bit/obs/perf), evidence, determinism class, L-level.

Acceptance: G3 requirement — Init/Std elaborate end-to-end from source with every builtin either native-behind-its-row or library-code source-elaborated, zero upstream implementation executing; census diff tooling ready for epoch advances; the G0-8 resistance list resolved row by row. Tests: census-totality CI check; facade-stub compile checks against real metaprogram code; per-row differential fixture families.

title: W6: Mirror conformance rig — real tactic code differentials, T2/T3 tiers, PG-M
description:
Stand up the Mirror conformance rig — the metaprogram-corpus differential (§18.2): the ecosystem REAL tactic and metaprogram code (corpus family C2: packages chosen for metaprogramming/FFI/Lake/syntax weight) executed on Golem against the native facade, with environments, InfoTrees, diagnostics, and generated names diffed against the oracle runs — nightly, randomized workloads included. This rig is where the census totality claim and every facade row L-level are EARNED, continuously.

Also in scope: the T2/T3 differential elaboration tiers over the Corpus (T2: acceptance + diagnostics positions/severities + statement-level environment identity; T3: term-level identity up to registered normalization — alpha, mdata policy — with residual divergences triaged defect-first); comparison classes as versioned normalizer code; the declaration differential cross-checking both implementations outputs through Crucible AND fln-checker; PG-M measurement (metaprogram corpus wall <= 1.25x Reference at G4, <= 0.9x at G6 — native facade beating hosted dispatch is the design bet, measured not assumed).

Acceptance: the rig runs nightly with a divergence ledger where every entry is classified (fidelity defect with owner, or Behavior-Note candidate with evidence); T2 = 100% on the gated corpus at G4; PG-M trajectory published. Tests: the rig self-tests via seeded facade defects (a perturbed facade row must surface as a divergence, proving detection power); trace-replay integration with the golden-trace lanes.

title: W7: simp faithful core + deterministic compiled rewrite-index format
description:
Implement the simp faithful core + compiled rewrite indexes (§12.1, §12.2): the Reference simp semantics — simp-set discipline, discrimination-tree candidate retrieval, congruence closure interplay, dsimp/simp-only behaviors, attribute-driven extension — implemented natively end to end (driver, congruence interplay, extension dispatch are Anvil code behind obs-class Mirror facade rows), with the Corpus simp-heavy files plus instrumented-oracle rewrite traces as the fidelity corpus. Mathlib simp EXTENSIONS and simprocs are user code on Golem calling the native driver through the facade.

Compiled rewrite indexes (§12.2): per-library discrimination automata compiled at Ledger commit time and shipped as content-addressed CAS artifacts (an olean-next attachment) — mathlib tens of thousands of simp lemmas become a preloaded matcher, not a per-session index build. Staleness is impossible BY CONSTRUCTION (content-addressed against the simp-set own hash).

Acceptance: simp outcomes match the oracle on the simp-heavy fidelity corpus (rewrite firings, order where observable, result terms); index-backed retrieval produces identical candidate sets to freshly-built trees (representation-equivalence tests); index build determinism (same simp-set hash, same artifact bytes). Tests: golden rewrite-trace replay; dsimp/simp-only behavioral fixtures; congruence-interplay differentials; a stale-index mutation test (perturbed simp-set with unperturbed index must be structurally unloadable).

title: W7: norm_num + omega — native certificate-producing arithmetic cores
description:
Implement norm_num/omega native cores (§12.4): norm_num-class evaluation and the omega decision procedure with native cores (fln-bignum-backed), certificate-producing, kernel-replayed — every result enters the environment only through a kernel-checked artifact (FL-INV-06; the engines are untrusted by constitution, which is what makes native reimplementation safe).

title: W7: Verdict — owned CDCL SAT with owned proof logging and checker
description:
Implement Verdict (fln-verdict, §12.5): an owned CDCL SAT solver — watched literals, VSIDS/CHB-class heuristics, clause management, inprocessing kept modest and honest — with OWNED proof logging and an OWNED independent certificate checker: the bv_decide pipeline bit-blasts through Verdict, Verdict emits a checkable unsatisfiability certificate, and a small independent checker (kernel-adjacent in audit rigor, separate in code) validates it before the reflected proof is admitted. The external-binary TCB (CaDiCaL + LRAT toolchain, weakness census #7) dies here; Behavior Note BN-06 records the change (same theorems, owned certificate chain, no external solver).

Parity posture (risk R17 honesty): correctness is absolute from day one; competitiveness is a ratchet — PG-9 tracks Verdict against the Reference solver on the bv_decide corpus with a published gap budget (100% agreement; wall <= 2x Reference-with-CaDiCaL at G5, ratcheting thereafter). If the gap persists, it PUBLISHES honestly; semantics never depended on solver speed.

Acceptance: 100% verdict agreement on the bv_decide corpus; every unsat certificate validated by the independent checker before admission; PG-9 wall within budget at G5. Tests: SAT/UNSAT differential vs known-good results on standard benchmarks + the bv_decide corpus; certificate-checker independence verified structurally (no shared solver modules); fuzzed CNF + fuzzed bit-blasting inputs under budgets; a planted bogus-certificate must be rejected.

title: W7: grind reimplementation — congruence + e-matching, honestly staged L-levels
description:
Reimplement grind natively (§12.6): grind is core-toolchain machinery at the pin, so under the Oracle-Only Law it is reimplemented — congruence closure, e-matching, and its theory hooks as Anvil engines behind facade rows — developed against instrumented-oracle decision traces and Reference-as-oracle differentials.

Staging is deliberate and honest: its Parity-Ledger rows ENTER at L1/L2 and ratchet to L3 on the grind corpus by G4 rather than pretending day-one exactness, because grind semantics are still moving upstream and the census pins our coupling per epoch. Mathlib-layer automation (aesop, polyrith, ...) is user code on Golem and needs NO reimplementation. All outputs kernel-checked per FL-INV-06.

Acceptance: the grind corpus at L3 by G4 (goal-closure agreement with classified exclusions); decision-trace replay green on the covered fragment; epoch-diff tooling flags upstream grind changes as census deltas requiring row review. Tests: congruence-closure property tests (equivalence-class correctness vs a simple oracle implementation); e-matching trigger fixtures from the traces; differential goal families with the exclusion ledger explicit.

title: W7: Frontier lanes — e-graph saturation + tactic portfolios, shadow-run promotion
description:
Implement the frontier rewrite lanes (§12.3, §12.7): the e-graph equality-saturation lane over simp-set subsets with proof extraction (rewrites recorded as justifications, extracted proofs replayed through the ordinary lemma-application path, kernel-checked like everything else — honestly scoped: conditional rewrites and binder-crossing rules restrict the eligible subset; the lane is an accelerator with a completeness fallback to the faithful core, never a replacement; its win condition is MEASURED via the PG-A profile, not assumed), and speculative tactic portfolios (first-with-work-stealing, deterministic winner selection by registered CGSE policy, losers cancelled through their asupersync regions; snapshot-cheap try trees — the search-side dividend of O(1) environment snapshots, exposed as tactics and through Envoy).

Both lanes promote ONLY by shadow-run (§12.6/§18.9): dual-run on the same demand nodes, products diffed, divergence and latency distributions recorded, promotion per workload/epoch/platform cell with deterministic sampling continuing afterward and auto-demotion on any soundness, fidelity, or tail-latency breach — promotion is a matrix, never a global flag. Frontier features never leak into faithful/sound artifacts (D18).

Acceptance: e-graph lane produces kernel-checked results agreeing with the faithful core on its eligible subset, with the measured win condition published; portfolio winner selection deterministic under the lab at any thread count; shadow-run promotion machinery generic enough for the other promotion consumers (K2 optimizations, Iron tiers, epoch bridges). Tests: extraction-proof replay fixtures; eligibility-boundary fixtures (ineligible rewrites must route to the faithful core); cancellation-correctness under portfolio racing (obligation-leak oracle clean); auto-demotion drill (planted breach must demote and file the incident bundle).

Shadow-run data is evidence, not a substitute for the kernel-checked extraction law.

title: W8: Ledger records + CAS — demand nodes, five planes, retention tiers
description:
Implement Ledger records + the CAS store (§13.1): every declaration commit produces a record — decl_hash = H(name, elaborated statement, elaborated value/body class, direct dep hashes, options-that-matter, toolchain epoch, mode) — stored content-addressed (frankensqlite metadata + CAS blobs) with dependency edges as first-class rows. The environment at any point is a Merkle frontier keyed by logical roots; a module is a view; a build is a query.

The record generalizes to a demand node — kind, implementation version, epoch, semantic inputs, declared reads/effects (the §10.6 vocabulary), capability inputs, output schema, determinism class D0-D4 — spanning five interlocking planes with separate retention: source, language, trust (kernel/checker receipts), build (facets, codegen, links), service (LSP products, indexes). An LSP hover can be evicted without touching the checked declaration beneath it; retention tiers T0 (trusted roots, never sole-copy-evicted) -> T4 (observational, sampled) with eviction weighing recomputation cost, fan-out, and pressure.

Acceptance: record creation + retrieval on real elaboration output; plane separation demonstrated (service-plane eviction leaves trust plane intact); logical-root stability across hosts. Tests: hash-input completeness property tests (any semantic input change changes the hash; any excluded input does not); retention-tier eviction policy fixtures; frankensqlite crash-recovery drills on the metadata store (WAL semantics honest under kill -9).

title: W8: Incremental law — true-cone invalidation, early cutoff, perturbation-validated matrix
description:
Implement the incremental law + invalidation matrix (§13.2): an edit invalidates exactly the records whose hash inputs changed — the TRUE dependency cone computed on recorded edges (franken_networkx dominator/reachability machinery), not the import-file cone. Whitespace, comments, docstrings (non-doc facets), reordering of independent declarations, and edits to unrelated declarations in an imported file invalidate NOTHING downstream. FL-INV-05 (a Ledger hit is semantically identical to recomputation) is enforced by the conservative rule set — anything not proven irrelevant is relevant — plus a standing Tribunal rig that randomly recomputes hits and diffs.

Two refinements make the law sharp: EARLY CUTOFF (a re-run node whose output digest is unchanged stops the cascade — the salsa/Adapton discipline, with statement-hash vs value-hash separation doing the work: an opaque proof-body change invalidates the proof receipt and codegen closure, not its typed dependents); and the PUBLISHED invalidation matrix per change class (trivia-only -> source maps; proof-body edit -> that decl check + receipt + executable closure; type edit -> typed dependents; instance add/remove -> affected class-head search keys via the §10.4 negative-cache dependency keys; notation change -> GrammarEpoch descendants after the activation point; option change -> declared readers; plugin change -> classified reads + ABI load products) — each with its conservative fallback and each PERTURBATION-VALIDATED (mutate an allegedly-irrelevant input, require bit-identical products, demote to opaque on failure). Answer identity vs trace identity is explicit.

Risk R13 (stale hit) law: any confirmed stale hit disables the implicated rule class globally (suffix-barrier fallback) until proven, with a public incident bundle. Acceptance: PG-3 (leaf-edit p50 <= 15 s, p95 <= 90 s warm) trajectory; metamorphic invalidate-nothing suite green; random-recompute rig running. Tests: the C8 edit-sequence corpus with expected cones; perturbation campaigns; clean-rebuild-equivalence.

title: W8: Lake surface — lakefile.lean on Golem, dual rebuild decisions, transactional resolution
description:
Implement the Lake surface (fln-lake, §13.3): lake CLI-compatible — lakefile.lean EXECUTES on Golem (the configuration DSL is just Lean; user code, so Golem executing it IS the compatibility mechanism), lakefile.toml takes the fast path, targets/facets map onto Ledger queries, require fetches via the D2 git tool through the shared subprocess capsule, lean-toolchain and elan layout honored so elan multiplexes FrankenLean toolchains transparently. Exit codes, --json, and the query surface match the pin.

The behavior upgrade is sound-mode and Behavior-Noted (BN-03): decl-granular no-op detection, with --faithful-invalidation restoring file-cone behavior for byte-for-byte CI reproduction studies. Internally TWO rebuild decisions are computed every time: the Reference decision (what the pin Lake would rebuild — faithful mode never omits an output upstream would produce, though it may satisfy it from a valid Ledger record and still project expected files/traces) and the native decision (which demand nodes actually changed); fln build explain <target> shows both, the delta, changed input identities, opaque barriers, and cache outcomes. Dependency resolution is transactional (a failed fetch never leaves a half-rewritten manifest presented as complete) and emits a resolution receipt; hermeticity is reported on the H0-H4 ladder, never overclaimed.

This bead also OWNS open question OQ-9: whether a native git-protocol client eventually replaces the D2 git tool, or git stays a permanent optional external — resolved here with the decision recorded in the plan decision log (the default posture is permanent D2 status, matching upstream Lake own contract; a native client is only justified if the closed-universe benefit outweighs the protocol-maintenance cost).

Acceptance: real Lake projects (incl. mathlib layout) configure and build unmodified; the Lake transcript corpus (C6) diffs clean on CLI surfaces; fln build explain output validated on fixture edits; OQ-9 decision recorded. Tests: lakefile.lean execution differentials; interrupted-fetch drills (C6 interrupted updates); manifest-atomicity fault drills; exit-code/JSON transcript fixtures.

title: W8: Cache federation + epoch bridges — validated atp CAS, conservative cross-epoch reuse
description:
Implement cache federation + epoch-stable caching (§13.5, §13.6): the CAS syncs over atp (multi-donor, fountain-coded, resumable) or HTTPS fallback, entries signed and verified; a fln cache shim preserves the lake exe cache muscle memory and hydrates from the community existing archives during transition. Because records are keyed semantically, a cache populated by ANY machine on the epoch serves every project shape — first-mathlib-build becomes: sync frontier, elaborate your delta (PG-10: hydration >= 2x lake exe cache get on the lossy-path fixture, >= 1x clean).

Security law (R18): a remote entry is UNTRUSTED DATA until validated locally — node key and schema, content hashes and closure, epoch/platform compatibility, capability-input binding, kernel/checker receipts where relevant, signature policy, decompression bounds; a malformed entry becomes a miss plus a security event, never trusted-state poison; concurrent fill is lease-suppressed; corruption quarantines are first-class records.

Epoch-stable caching: toolchain bumps re-key the epoch, but the Ledger records WHY — a pin advance ships a machine-diffed census, and records provably untouched by the delta may carry epoch bridges (frontier, off by default, conservative allowlist, gated behind its own soundness rig — OQ-6 owns allowlist v1). The migration autopilot (audit project against the new epoch census, propose rewrites from the repair classes, lockstep builds, apply-transaction with rollback; never edits without explicit apply) is specified here and consumes the Bloodhound repair engine (W11).

Acceptance: PG-10 on the WAN fixtures; validation rejects every corpus of malformed entries; bridge soundness rig green on allowlist v1. Tests: multi-donor pull under injected loss; poisoned-entry fixtures; lease-suppression races under the lab; quarantine-and-revalidate drills.

It does not claim the end-user migration autopilot before Bloodhound and the epoch ratchet exist. The complete audit/propose/verify/explicit-apply/rollback workflow is owned by franken_lean-epoch-migration-autopilot-q9w; this bead supplies its bridge identities, validation facts, and rollback-capable cache primitives.

title: W8: Watch mode + distributed elaboration — local re-verification, no distributed trust
description:
Implement watch mode + distributed elaboration (§13.7): lake build --watch (filesystem events -> cone recompute -> live frankentui progress with dependency-cone visualization) and distributed elaboration — remote workers pulling Ledger tasks over asupersync RPC, results RE-VERIFIED by local Crucible on arrival: distribution without distributed trust (workers may lie; results validate locally; disagreement blocks and never votes — the same trust model Envoy remote check service reuses, §16.3).

Acceptance: watch mode recomputes exactly the invalidation-matrix cone on real edit streams with the frankentui surface live; distributed builds produce bit-identical Ledger states to local builds (FL-INV-01 extends across the wire); a lying-worker fixture (corrupted result) is caught by local re-verification and quarantined. Tests: filesystem-event storm fixtures (rapid saves, atomic-rename editors); worker-loss and partial-result drills; determinism differential local-vs-distributed on the corpus; cancellation of in-flight remote tasks drains cleanly (obligation-leak oracle).

title: W9: Lantern wire + daemon — byte-compatible LSP dialect, one shared import heap
description:
Implement the Lantern wire dialect + daemon topology (§14.1, §14.2): LSP with the $/lean/* extensions — plainGoal, plainTermGoal, the rpc/* session protocol (connect/call/release, reference-holding semantics), waitForDiagnostics, semantic tokens with the Reference legend, diagnostics shapes, widget delivery (client executes JS, wire-compatible pass-through) — BYTE-compatible so vscode-lean4 and every other client connects to lean --server and cannot tell.

Topology: the watchdog/worker PROCESS model is not replicated — only its observable behavior is. One daemon, all open files, all projects on a toolchain epoch, one shared immutable import heap (§7.5): the ~60 s x N-workers x GBs x N multiplication becomes once-per-daemon (PG-4: warm attach <= 2 s p50; PG-6: steady RSS serving >= 8 open mathlib files <= 1.3x ONE Reference worker). Per-file fault isolation becomes per-session region isolation: a crashing elaboration cancels an asupersync region, not a process; the supervisor restarts the session with the same client-visible semantics as a worker restart (BN-04). Transport is asupersync plumbing; zero protocol code written here.

Acceptance: vscode-lean4 connects unmodified and passes the smoke matrix basics; recorded-session replays (C5) diff clean at the wire with registered nondeterminism classes normalized; PG-4/PG-6 trajectory measured. Tests: wire-level golden transcripts; rpc session-lifetime fixtures (reference holding/release); region-crash isolation drills (one session dies, others unaffected, client sees worker-restart semantics).

title: W9: Snapshots + machine session API — publication generations, O(1) proof-state forks
description:
Implement snapshots + incrementality + the machine session API (§14.3, §14.4): every open document version maps to an immutable DocumentSnapshot (uri, version, content id, project logical root, GrammarEpochs at boundaries, command transactions, publication generation); edits derive new snapshots; superseded requests keep their snapshot but LOSE publication authority — the stale-diagnostics-into-a-new-buffer race is structurally impossible; every outward message rechecks its generation first.

Incrementality: command-granular snapshots extended DOWNWARD — Quill sub-command reparse + Athanor dataflow means an edit inside a proof re-elaborates from the finest enclosing checkpoint (tactic-block granularity where upstream incrementality already reaches; declaration floor everywhere else), against O(1) environment snapshots, cancel-correct under typing bursts (PG-L: edit-ack + stale-cancel <= 25 ms p95; verified first-goal after mid-proof edit <= 100 ms p50). Diagnostics deduplicate by stable identity (typed cause + primary origin), not string equality; code actions carry idempotency keys + preconditions (stale-goal actions rejected with a structured response, never applied optimistically); progressive proof-state negotiation ($/frankenlean/progressiveProofState: ParseReady -> ProvisionalGoals -> VerifiedGoals -> ImpactComplete, provisional never cached as trusted); multi-project sessions bind each root to its epoch capsule.

The machine session API (§14.4): fork a proof state, apply a tactic, read goals, diff environments — O(1) per branch, thousands of branches per daemon; the substrate Envoy sells and the number that embarrasses the 18-735 s/branch literature. Acceptance: PG-L ladder met on the warm corpus; race-freedom verified under lab typing storms; session API forks measured at O(1). Tests: DPOR exploration of edit/cancel/publish interleavings; stale-precondition fixtures; generation-recheck mutation test (a message skipping the recheck must be impossible or caught).

title: W9: Replay bundles + protocol rigs — deterministic server bugs, editor smoke matrix
description:
Implement session replay bundles + protocol rigs (§14.3, §18.2): every session can emit a replay bundle (init transcript, edit and request sequence, content ids or sealed sources, scheduling profile, normalized outbound transcript); fln server replay bundle reproduces the session and reports the FIRST divergence — server bugs become deterministic objects, not race anecdotes.

Protocol rigs: recorded editor sessions replayed against BOTH servers (FrankenLean and the Reference) with wire-level diffing under registered nondeterminism classes (request interleaving) normalized by versioned normalizer code; the vscode-lean4 smoke matrix in CI (open, edit, hover, goals, completion, go-to-def, widgets, waitForDiagnostics across representative projects incl. a mathlib slice); the C5 corpus grown continuously from real usage.

Acceptance: replay determinism — a bundle replays bit-identically under the lab runtime; the smoke matrix green across the editor scenarios; first-divergence reporting demonstrated on a seeded server mutant. Tests: bundle round-trip fixtures; normalizer-honesty tests (a normalizer may never discard an error body to pass — planted-divergence detection); torture under the asupersync lab: cancellation storms, fault injection, crash-recovery of session state with seed replay (§18.2 torture lane).

title: W9: Palimpsest — event journal, causal proof graph, the welding law
description:
Implement the Palimpsest substrate + causal proof graph (§15.1): structured elaboration events — unifier attempts, instance candidates, macro expansions, scheduler decisions, cache hits, kernel verdicts — recorded per declaration into ring-buffered traces (always-on cheap tier) and full archives (opt-in, frankensqlite-backed), keyed to Syntax ranges. Recording NEVER changes results (observer discipline, enforced by dual-run rigs: traced and untraced runs must produce identical products).

From the journal, the causal proof graph (Bet B7): typed nodes (source ranges, commands, expansions, declarations, instances, searches, tactic steps, kernel/checker verdicts, extension entries, build facets, capability sessions, branches/merges) and typed EDGE FAMILIES (IMPORTS; READS_TYPE vs READS_VALUE vs UNFOLDS; SELECTS_INSTANCE/REJECTS_INSTANCE; USES_SIMP_RULE; EXPANDS_FROM/GENERATED_BY; READS_GRAMMAR/WRITES_GRAMMAR; PROVES/CHECKED_BY/CROSS_CHECKED_BY; COMPILES_TO/EXPORTS_SYMBOL/INITIALIZES_BEFORE; READS_FILE/SPAWNS_TOOL/LOADS_LIBRARY; INVALIDATES/REUSES; SUGGESTED_BY; MIGRATES_TO/REPAIRS), every edge carrying span, phase, policy/epoch, receipt id, and a COMPLETENESS CLASS: Complete (perturbation-tested), Conservative (may over-approximate, must not miss), Observed, Opaque, Inferred.

THE WELDING LAW (stated once, enforced everywhere): only Complete and Conservative edges may drive Ledger invalidation or any trust decision; Observed/Inferred may rank, explain, suggest — never invalidate. Impact cones (interface, definitional, instance/search, syntax, executable, evidence, conservative-potential) computed from deltas — the same cones the Ledger matrix consumes and reviewers read as release risk. Storage: franken_networkx in-process over frankensqlite journals; optional frankengraphdb export is frontier, derived, never load-bearing. Acceptance: observer-discipline dual-run rig green; cone computation validated against the invalidation matrix fixtures; completeness-class enforcement CI check (an Observed edge reaching the invalidation path must be structurally impossible). Tests: journal crash-recovery; graph rebuild-from-journal determinism; export redaction fixtures (§15.3c privacy law).

title: W9: Forensics, semantic diff/merge, why-trusts, time travel, SQL
description:
Implement the forensics + semantic diff/merge surfaces (§15.2, §15.3, §15.3b, §15.3c, §15.4, §15.5): unification forensics (sound-mode failures report the MINIMAL DISAGREEMENT — smallest subterm pair, transparency in force, approximation stage that gave up, assignment trail — rendered as a diff, not a wall; faithful renders upstream text verbatim); instance forensics (the Synod why-not tree with a one-line head above the expandable tree); both projections of one mechanism — the semantic blame slice (minimal or near-minimal-honestly-labeled causal subgraph, stable node ids: robots get the typed slice, humans get the rendering).

Semantic diff (fln diff): levels source -> syntax -> interface -> proof -> reduction -> extension -> executable -> evidence, so "proof-only change" is a CHECKABLE claim. Semantic merge: branches merge automatically when the graph proves their transactions commute AND the combined declarations re-check through Crucible; text-conflict-but-semantically-independent proposes the patch; text-clean-but-semantics-collide (instance/simp/notation effects) BLOCKS the silent merge and explains — the failure mode Git cannot see; nothing merges around the kernel. Fragility signals (§15.3c): advisory-only (D3), constitutionally barred from Crucible. fln why-trusts: the trust cone walk (axioms, sorryAx, native_decide receipts + tiers, extern/plugin surfaces, --trust extensions). Time travel: goal-state scrubbing, fln replay <decl> from the recorded closure, repro bundles packing a bug input closure. Build forensics: the SQL surface (fln doctor --sql + shipped report views).

Acceptance: forensic outputs validated on curated failure corpora (unification and instance failures with known causes); merge-conflict fixtures demonstrate the semantic-block on Git-clean merges; why-trusts output matches hand-computed cones. Tests: blame-slice minimality checks on seeded contradictions; diff-level property tests (a proof-only edit shows empty interface diff); merge commute-proof mutation tests; replay determinism fixtures.

title: W10: Iron backends — owned native codegen + JIT, C backend as parity apparatus
description:
Implement the Iron backends (§17.3, §11.3): the sovereign path — FLBC to native objects/executables via an owned baseline code generator (NO LLVM, no cranelift, no cc), x86-64 + aarch64 first, so lake build of executables needs no external toolchain (the G6 headline; resolves weakness census #12 partially and the mandatory-cc contract entirely). Iron-JIT (frontier, standard-mode only, fln-unsafe-jit): a baseline template JIT from FLBC with inline caches burned in — the tier that makes hot tactic loops approach precompiled speed; certified builds never enable it; results bit-identical to the interpreter by construction, spot-checked by a standing dual-run rig (PG-7: JIT >= 0.5x Reference-precompiled on the corpus).

Also: --backend c — emit C in the Reference idiom and drive the USER toolchain exactly as upstream leanc does (D2 tool #1, optional), for parity studies and exotic targets; never the anchor (D16). Both backends dual-run governed: Iron pinned to interpreter results, interpreter pinned to Reference observable evaluation semantics. OQ-4 (target set beyond x86-64/aarch64; JIT/static emitter sharing) is resolved inside this bead and recorded in the decision log.

Acceptance: real executables (incl. a Lake-built project binary) produced by Iron run correctly with zero cc present; JIT dual-run rig green; C-backend output compiles under the Reference leanc contract on the parity corpus. Tests: differential execution interpreter-vs-Iron-vs-C-backend on the corpus; executable-page management ledger rows audited (fln-unsafe-jit, D3); JIT-vs-interpreter bit-identity spot checks in CI; cross-arch CI lanes.

title: W10: Outbound ABI door — our plugins in the Reference host, per-platform matrix
description:
Implement the outbound ABI door (§17.4, §6.6, risk R5): FrankenLean-built plugins loading into the REFERENCE host — the harder direction of the bidirectional plugin promise, staged deliberately to G5 with its own Tribunal matrix per platform (inbound landed with W2).

Why harder: the Reference host has internal assumptions (allocator behavior, initialization ordering, symbol resolution details) we do not control; our emitted artifacts must satisfy them without any cooperation from upstream. Method: build against the generated ABI contract; validate through the C4 cross-load matrix outbound lanes; every discovered host assumption becomes a contract row (or an honest per-platform tier in the Parity Ledger rather than blocking G5 — the R5 fallback).

Acceptance: FrankenLean-built precompileModules artifacts and @[extern] libraries load and run in the Reference toolchain on the covered platforms; the outbound matrix green per platform or tiered honestly; mixed pipelines (Reference host importing our plugin importing our oleans) exercised. Tests: the outbound C4 lanes per platform; symbol-resolution and initializer-ordering fixtures in the Reference host; a soak with the Reference test suite driving our plugin surfaces.

title: W11: Bloodhound — verified search, premise retrieval, proof repair, counterfactuals
description:
Implement Bloodhound (fln-hound, §16.1): the candidate-production system under one law — every candidate is untrusted text/terms until Athanor elaborates and Crucible accepts it (ranking is UX, proofs are proofs; determinism class D3, never touching the trust story).

Scope: in-toolchain hybrid search (frankensearch two-tier: lexical + semantic, bundled embedder, no network/Python/service) over statements, names, docstrings, simp/instance membership, indexed INCREMENTALLY at Ledger commit; exact?/apply?-class premise retrieval as a candidate generator verified by actual elaboration; Loogle-style structural patterns on a type-shape fingerprint lane; CandidateCards (source/term, provenance + retrieval channels, required imports/instances, rank components, elaboration outcome, kernel outcome, fragility signals, replay identity) in three grades — initial (low-latency lexical/type-head/local-context), refined (structural unification, graph ranking over Palimpsest cones, prior-use paths, optional learned rerank), verified (kernel-accepted under the exact snapshot); a failed refinement never withdraws a valid initial result.

Proof repair (the migration autopilot engine): diff old/new environment + extension graphs, find the earliest failed constraint cell, re-elaborate with holes around the blame slice, search replacements (renames, binder-order shifts, bridge lemmas, instance-priority shifts, simp-set drift, termination adjustments via TerminationCards, import moves), validate each minimal patch through the FULL pipeline, rank by semantic change size + fragility delta, emit a patch transaction with rollback. Counterfactual analysis (replay under altered policy on branch-local snapshots) and minimization (declared objective vectors, trade-offs shown). Learned components (frankentorch tier): local-first, identity on every card, candidates-only, removal leaves a complete deterministic baseline.

Acceptance: search quality measured on a query corpus; repair success rate measured on a broken-proof corpus derived from real mathlib migrations; every repair kernel-validated. Tests: index incrementality (commit -> queryable within the latency budget); candidate-grade monotonicity fixtures; repair rollback drills; learned-tier removal leaves baseline green.

title: W11: Folio — native docs facet, receipt-bound pages, incremental rendering
description:
Implement Folio (fln-doc, §16.2): doc-gen4-surface-compatible documentation as a Ledger facet — docstring markdown through franken_markdown, mathematics through fmd-math rendered natively to HTML and PDF (zero browser-side math stack), syntax highlighting via a Quill-fed lexer, cross-reference and instance graphs via franken_networkx, INCREMENTAL (only changed declarations re-render — mathlib docs become minutes, local, offline).

Documents are receipt-bound: a rendered page embeds the logical root and receipt links for every checked excerpt, and a later source change marks the artifact STALE rather than leaving a verified badge lying (the Witness discipline of §20.4 applied to documentation). The frontier lane: franken_manim consumes Palimpsest event sequences to render animated proof explanations (goal transformations under a tactic, unification constraints resolving, induction structure, before/after of a repair) — explanatory projections that link to checked declarations and are never themselves certificates.

Acceptance: doc output for a mathlib slice matches the doc-gen4 surface conventions (URLs, anchors, structure) so existing links and tooling survive; incremental re-render touches only the changed-declaration cone; receipt-staleness demonstrated (edit a source, the page marks stale). Tests: rendering golden tests (HTML structure + PDF layout smoke); math-rendering fixtures vs known TeX corpus; incremental-facet metamorphic tests (doc-irrelevant edits re-render nothing); link-integrity checks across the generated set.

title: W11: Envoy — MCP tools, ProofStateSnapshot, macaroon authority, multi-agent orchestration
description:
Implement Envoy (fln-mcp, §16.3): the agent door — MCP tools over the Lantern session API: goal_state, apply_tactic (against forked snapshots, budgeted), search_lemmas (Bloodhound), check_term, eval (capability-scoped), get_certificate, ledger_query, trace_query (Palimpsest). Budgets (heartbeats, wall, memory, expanded weight) are first-class parameters; every tool result carries provenance; read tools are sound-mode; write-to-file tools are explicit and default-off.

The session primitive: ProofStateSnapshot — a versioned, schema-registered, canonical serialization of an elaboration point (goals with local contexts, metavariable assignments, environment logical root, journal position, option set) — ROUND-TRIPPABLE (a snapshot IS a resumable state), stable across releases, structurally shared with the live store. Authority is macaroon-scoped (asupersync tokens): a credential names readable environment roots, the budget envelope, the consensus policy it may request, and whether write-tools exist — attenuable delegation, every check receipted. Robot responses carry schema/epoch/profile versions, request + snapshot ids, resource facts, data grade (provisional/verified), evidence links; mutating tools carry idempotency keys + preconditions.

On top: transactional multi-agent orchestration — fork a branch per agent, assign disjoint impact regions from Palimpsest cones, stream scoped context packs, accept PATCH PROPOSALS (never direct trusted mutations), re-elaborate + kernel-check each, detect semantic conflicts via §15.3b, merge deterministically, emit a mission receipt with a rollback root — agent success measured by accepted kernel-checked commits. Plus the remote check service (sealed capsule delta in, full-policy consensus verdict + signed transparency-logged receipt out; workers may lie, results validate locally). Acceptance: the tool surface validated against a frozen JSON schema contract; snapshot round-trips bit-stable; a two-agent orchestration scenario end-to-end with a semantic-conflict block demonstrated. Tests: budget-exhaustion typed results (FL-INV-07 over the wire); macaroon attenuation fixtures (a narrowed token cannot widen); idempotency/precondition replay tests; schema-freeze contract tests.

title: W11: Terminal InfoView + WASM Judge — fln goals in tmux, kernel in a browser tab
description:
Implement the terminal InfoView + the WASM Judge (§16.4, §16.4b): fln goals — the live proof panel over SSH/tmux (where this ecosystem agents and operators actually live), attaching to Lantern over the SAME RPC the editor uses: goals, hover, and widgets as text/ANSI projections via frankentui diff-based rendering; franken_node JS host held as the exploratory path to full widget fidelity outside VS Code (OQ-7 owns the tier decision — wire-compatible pass-through remains the contract).

The WASM Judge (fln-wasm): K1 + fln-checker compiled to a single WASM artifact — sealed-capsule (.flnpack) verification in a browser tab: drag a capsule, watch the kernel re-check it against the same vectors with the same bits; receipt + transparency-log inclusion validation; declaration/dependency browsing over the embedded graph slice. Zero-install third-party verification as a public good — the strongest possible demonstration that the trusted core needs NOTHING from a host: no filesystem, no network, no allocator tricks, no platform libm. Tier-1 scope is the Judge; a full in-browser elaborator is explicitly tier-2-if-ever (OQ-12 owns packaging tier + size/perf budget).

Acceptance: fln goals renders live goal states in tmux against a real daemon session with sub-second update on edits; the WASM Judge verifies a real sealed mathlib-slice capsule in a stock browser with bit-identical vectors vs the native K1 run. Tests: frankentui rendering goldens (ANSI projections); RPC-parity tests (terminal view == editor view for the same snapshot); WASM/native vector-identity CI lane; capsule tamper fixtures (any byte flip must fail verification with a typed error).

design: OPEN-QUESTION CLOSEOUT — this bead owns OQ-12 in addition to OQ-7. Freeze the WASM Judge tier-1 package boundary, browser/capsule UI inclusion, artifact size and verification-time budgets, supported browser matrix, memory/resource limits, and offline integrity model; record the decision before release packaging. Full browser elaboration remains tier-2-if-ever and cannot inflate the Judge's TCB claim.

title: W12: Distribution — static binaries, elan layout, reproducible dual-builds, D8 release audit
description:
Implement distribution (§17.1, §22.2 W12): static binaries per platform (lean, leanc, lake, fln) with the elan-compatible release layout so a lean-toolchain line names a FrankenLean toolchain and elan multiplexes it transparently; the install script; side-by-side epoch installs; the version-line law — parse-compatible with the Reference (version/commit fields preserved) plus a separate machine-readable implementation field; fln identity --json (implementation commit, epoch, profile, lockfile, TCB hash, evidence freshness); an exact-reference transcript mode for conformance harnesses only. Compatibility never becomes deception (§17.1).

Reproducible releases: release binaries built TWICE in isolated builders and byte-compared (divergence blocks); --reproducible certified across the matrix (linux-x86-64, linux-aarch64, macos-aarch64; windows bit-certification a separate declared decision, D17); the stdlib fixpoint (elaborating Init/Std from clean state twice — and once more through a freshly rebuilt toolchain — produces bit-identical oleans and logical roots: the native-world descendant of the classic triple bootstrap). The D8 release check: CI proves release binaries contain no symbol, path, or capability by which the Reference could be located, spawned, or linked; the ORACLE_FALLBACK harness is compiled out and proven absent.

Acceptance: cold-machine install (C9: clean-machine, air-gapped, path/locale variation) yields a working toolchain that builds a real project; dual-build byte-identity green; elan integration verified against real elan. Tests: install-script matrix; C9 bootstrap/reproducibility corpus; release-content audit (symbol scan, capability scan); toolchain-switching fixtures via lean-toolchain files.

title: W12: Epoch ratchet — mechanical pin advances, census diffs, migration beads
description:
Implement the epoch-advance ratchet as tooling (§22.5, §13.6): the mechanical 8 steps — (1) fetch + hash the official release into the epoch lab; (2) regenerate every census on every platform (ABI, olean, extern, builtin, options, CLI, LSP schemas); (3) run the existing corpus differentially and mine new behavior into fixtures + fresh instrumented-oracle traces; (4) machine-diff the public/Meta API, artifact, ABI, CLI, Lake, LSP surfaces; (5) regenerate facade stubs and OPEN MIGRATION BEADS for every moved symbol; (6) close or classify every new divergence (unclassified BLOCKS the epoch claim); (7) run the unchanged downstream corpus under both epochs; (8) publish the capsule, evidence bundle, and migration report — then move the pin.

Cadence target: a new stable upstream release reaches R3 within four weeks and R4 by the following cycle, lag publicly visible on the dashboard (risk R14: the newest closed epoch stays R4 while later epochs ship R2/R3 previews — latest-parity never implied). Supported-epoch count: initial posture current + previous stable (OQ-10). Epoch bridges (frontier, allowlisted, separately gated) are the pressure-release valve keeping the treadmill from owning the roadmap.

Acceptance: the ratchet runs end-to-end against a SIMULATED epoch advance (a synthetic delta over the current pin) producing the capsule, the census diffs, the auto-opened migration beads, and the block-on-unclassified behavior; the lag dashboard renders from ledger data. Tests: census-diff correctness fixtures (planted deltas per surface must be caught and classified); bead-opening automation tests; block-on-unclassified enforcement test.

design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — resolve the D2 interaction explicitly. Release tooling must not spawn br. The ratchet produces a deterministic schema-versioned migration-issue manifest for every moved symbol and divergence, with exact operator/repository-harness br import instructions; applying it is an external maintenance action, not a FrankenLean runtime subprocess. This bead owns epoch acquisition, regeneration, differentials, surface diffs, facade regeneration, classification gates, downstream dual-epoch runs, capsule/report production, and the manifest. franken_lean-epoch-migration-autopilot-q9w consumes those outputs for project repair.

title: W12: Witness claim matrix + docs CI — evidence-gated wording, safe mode, auto-demotion
description:
Implement Witness — the claim-to-proof matrix + documentation CI (§20.4, Bet B8): every public claim (compatibility, soundness, determinism, performance, recovery, security) is a row — claim id, exact text or template, allowed evidence state (OBSERVED with a no-mock reproduction command | TARGETED | HYPOTHESIS | PROVEN with stated scope | BLOCKED | RETIRED), owner workstream, scope (epoch/platform/mode/corpus), required gate + artifact schemas, freshness predicate, forbidden stronger wording, fallback text.

Documentation CI parses the docs against the matrix and REJECTS wording stronger than the evidence permits; coverage counts are generated from the ledgers, never hand-maintained; a benchmark is OBSERVED, never PROVEN; a Crucible-checked theorem is PROVEN relative to the assumptions its receipt names. Companion machinery: SAFE MODE — on InternalFault, checker disagreement, or corruption detection, the affected process fails closed, quarantines its caches, and requires independent revalidation before its outputs are trusted again; AUTO-DEMOTION — a promoted lane breaching thresholds demotes itself and files the incident bundle. The system explains its own behavior with the same rigor it demands of proofs.

Also here: the public Parity Ledger dashboard and the Behavior Notes register (BN-01..BN-08 seeded from §18.8, grown by evidence) as published, generated artifacts. Acceptance: docs CI catches a seeded overclaim (planted wording stronger than the matrix state must fail the build); safe-mode drills quarantine and revalidate; the dashboard regenerates from ledger data alone. Tests: matrix-schema validation; freshness-expiry fixtures (a stale OBSERVED claim must demote to its fallback text); auto-demotion incident-bundle fixtures.

design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — seed and validate Behavior Notes BN-01 through BN-09, including BN-09 for --trust journaling (the plan references BN-09 at §17.1 even though §18.8's seed paragraph stops at BN-08). Witness schema/docs CI can be built early, but this bead closes only after its runtime companions exist: consensus faults, Ledger quarantine/revalidation, and shadow-run auto-demotion. Its new dependencies make that closure honest.

title: Gate G1: The Independent Judge — all of mathlib re-checked, zero divergence, PG-1
description:
Convergence gate G1 "The Independent Judge" (§22.3): Marrow + Grimoire + Crucible check EVERY mathlib olean at the pin with zero acceptance divergence against the Reference toolchain; PG-1 met (<= 5 min on the 32-core profile, <= 25 min single-thread); fln check-olean ships as a standalone auditor.

Why this gate is first and why it matters strategically: the program FIRST public artifact is the strongest independent verification the Lean world has ever had — a foreign-blooded kernel (no shared code, no shared compiler, no shared bignum) re-checking the entire corpus in minutes — shipped BEFORE FrankenLean has elaborated a single file. It is simultaneously the deepest de-risking event available: it proves the ABI/olean/kernel spine (the layout extraction methodology, the region codec, the judgment inventory) against the full Corpus at production scale.

Evidence bundle required to declare the gate: full-corpus run logs on the baseline machine; receipts + transparency log entries; the kernel differential rigs green (lean4checker/lean4lean agreement); Parity Ledger rows for the kernel/olean/ABI surfaces at their achieved L-levels; PG-1 and PG-8 numbers with claim states; every divergence (if any occurred) closed through the §8.2 triage law with fixtures.

title: Gate G2: The Golem Wakes — user code runs, plugins load, PG-7 floor
description:
Convergence gate G2 "The Golem Wakes" (§22.3): user-class compiled Lean code executes on Golem with #eval/IO/Task parity on the fixture corpus; Reference-built plugins load and run (ABI door, inbound); initialization ordering proven; PG-7 interpreter floor met on early corpora; the intrinsic census fully populated with fixtures.

This gate proves the execution half of the Native Mirror bet: the VM value-identity claim (FLBC values ARE ABI objects — one calling convention across interpreted code, native intrinsics, and foreign plugins) holds for the code class Golem will actually run in production (mathlib tactics, lakefiles, #eval bodies). It is the prerequisite for W6 tactic-framework work at scale and for the Lake surface (lakefile.lean execution).

Evidence bundle: the parity corpus green under both the production effect plane and the lab runtime; real plugin load/run transcripts with initialization-DAG receipts; C4 inbound matrix results per platform; PG-7 measurements against the W1 baseline with claim states; RC-balance and ownership-shadow soak results.

title: Gate G3: The Mirror Holds — Init/Std from source, T2 identity, fixpoint bit-stable
description:
Convergence gate G3 "The Mirror Holds" (§22.3): the native toolchain elaborates Init and Std end-to-end FROM SOURCE — every builtin either native behind its facade row or library-code source-elaborated, zero upstream implementation executing anywhere — with T2 identity against the Reference, the stdlib double-elaboration fixpoint bit-stable, and the metaprogram-corpus rig (curated mathlib-tactic slice through the facade) green at its declared L-levels. The ORACLE_FALLBACK compile-out check passes on the gate build.

This is the gate that proves the central bet (B2): the parser, elaborator, instance engine, tactic framework, and scheduler reproduce the Reference at stdlib scale, and real ecosystem metaprogram code cannot distinguish the native facade from the machinery it replaced. It is the last gate before the full Corpus becomes the target.

Evidence bundle: T2 differential results over Init/Std (acceptance, diagnostics, statement-level environment identity); the fixpoint runs (bit-identical oleans + logical roots across double elaboration and a freshly rebuilt toolchain); metaprogram-rig results with the facade-row L-level table; census-totality check output; the D8 compile-out proof on the gate binaries; PG-5 thread-matrix results at stdlib scale.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

title: Gate G4: The Corpus Whole — full mathlib, T2=100%, PG-2, mixed builds
description:
Convergence gate G4 "The Corpus Whole" (§22.3): full mathlib elaboration — T2 = 100%, T3 measured with a published ratchet, PG-2(G4) met (wall <= 0.5x Reference, same machine/threads), olean outputs consumed by the Reference toolchain in the mixed-build matrix. This is the gate that makes "an implementation of Lean" a fact rather than a claim: the Corpus is the de-facto behavioral specification (§1.9), and elaborating all of it with statement-identical environments is the definition of having implemented the language.

Also folded in per §22.2/§12.6: grind rows at L3 on the grind corpus; simp fidelity at scale; the Ledger incremental law live under the full Corpus (the PG-3 measurement baseline); native_decide Tier 1 as the sound-mode default; PG-M (metaprogram corpus <= 1.25x Reference).

Evidence bundle: the full T2/T3 differential ledger with every T3 residual classified (fidelity defect closed, or documented free choice with pinned CGSE policy); PG-2/PG-M numbers with claim states; the mixed-producer matrix transcripts (our oleans under the Reference, theirs under ours, interleaved builds); the R2 divergence ledger empty or published as errata with quantified blast radius (the epoch ships R3, not R4, until closed).
