title: Gate G5: The Daemon — vscode-lean4 unmodified, PG-4/5/6/L, Verdict PG-9
description:
Convergence gate G5 "The Daemon" (§22.3): Lantern serves vscode-lean4 unmodified across the smoke matrix; PG-4 (attach), PG-5 (determinism), PG-6 (memory), PG-L (interactive latency ladder) met; the machine session API live; Verdict at PG-9; FrankenLean-built plugins load in the Reference (ABI door, outbound — or honestly tiered per platform per the R5 fallback).

This is the gate at which FrankenLean becomes a daily-driver: the editor experience (the ecosystem most-felt surface) is proven at wire fidelity and at interactive latency, the single-daemon shared-heap architecture delivers its promised arithmetic (once-per-daemon import replacing the per-worker multiplication), and the decision-procedure stack stands on owned components end to end.

Evidence bundle: the editor smoke matrix transcripts (open/edit/hover/goals/completion/widgets across representative projects incl. a mathlib slice); PG-4/5/6/L measurements with claim states and tail distributions; session-API load results (thousands of concurrent branches); PG-9 corpus agreement + wall numbers; outbound C4 matrix results or the per-platform tier table; 4-hour soak with zero leaks.

title: Gate G6: Sovereign & Beyond — no-cc executables, Ledger GA, R4 declared
description:
Convergence gate G6 "Sovereign & Beyond" (§22.3): Iron builds real executables with no C compiler; Ledger GA with cache federation and PG-3 (the cone) + PG-10 (cache hydration); the leapfrog surfaces shipped (Bloodhound, Folio, Envoy, terminal InfoView, WASM Judge); W12 distribution complete; --reproducible certified across the matrix. The toolchain is one static binary set that installs in seconds and owes the outside world nothing.

Also at this horizon per the gate table: PG-2 tightens to <= 0.35x Reference; PG-M to <= 0.9x (the native facade beating hosted dispatch — the design bet, measured); native_decide Tier 2 (trace-guided kernel replay) becomes the certified-path default (§11.6).

Evidence bundle: a cold-machine, air-gapped install building and checking a real project end to end (C9); Iron-built executables from real Lake projects with zero cc present; PG-3/PG-10 measurements on the WAN fixtures; the reproducible dual-build byte-identity results across the certified matrix; the R4 declaration for the pinned epoch — every mandatory Parity Ledger row at L4, no fallback code, the Witness matrix consistent with every published claim.

title: W1: Workspace scaffolding — §21 crate map, pinned nightly, unsafe-ledger CI, layering checks
description:
Scaffold the cargo workspace per the cycle-free crate map (§21): every fln-* crate stubbed with its one-line charter as crate-level docs — fln-core, fln-hash, fln-bignum, fln-libm, fln-rt, fln-unsafe-abi, fln-unsafe-region, fln-env, fln-olean, fln-kernel, fln-checker, fln-parse, fln-syntax, fln-elab, fln-comp, fln-vm, fln-unsafe-jit (feature iron), fln-anvil, fln-verdict, fln-ledger, fln-lake, fln-server, fln-trace, fln-hound, fln-doc, fln-mcp, fln-tui, fln-cli, fln (library facade), fln-conformance, fln-wasm — plus vendor/lean4-src at the pin (three D8 roles only, Apache-2.0 notices carried, never linked).

Structural enforcement from day one: rust-toolchain.toml pinning the exact nightly (no or-later); #![forbid(unsafe_code)] at every ordinary crate root; the three fln-unsafe-* boundary crates with #![deny(unsafe_code)] roots and the unsafe-boundary ledger file (path, invariant, evidence, safe fallback, no-claim boundary per row); CI checks that (a) walk the dependency graph and fail on any edge violating the strictly-downward §5 layering or the D3 structural laws (no fln-unsafe-* crate depends on fln-kernel/fln-checker; no unsafe crate exports a type launderable into a checked declaration), (b) reject any unledgered unsafe site, (c) enforce the fln-kernel line-count covenant once the crate has content.

Acceptance: cargo check --all-targets green on the stubbed workspace from a cold checkout; the layering CI check demonstrably fails on a seeded illegal edge (add a test-only violation in CI to prove detection, then remove); the unsafe-ledger check fails on a seeded unledgered allow site. Tests: the CI checks themselves plus a workspace-graph snapshot test that any new crate or edge must be explicitly acknowledged in a reviewed file.

title: W1: Quality gates — scripts/check.sh, CI pipeline, e2e smoke harness with detailed logging
description:
Build the quality-gate infrastructure AGENTS.md promises: scripts/check.sh running cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test in order, stopping on first failure — wired as the CI test step (never duplicated inline in workflow files), plus ubs integration on changed files and the e2e smoke harness.

The e2e smoke harness is the always-runnable end-to-end script family with GREAT, DETAILED LOGGING: each script logs every step (command, inputs, expected vs actual, timing, environment fingerprint) to both human-readable and NDJSON streams so failures are diagnosable from CI artifacts alone without reproduction. Initial smokes: workspace-builds-cold (clean checkout -> cargo build), contracts-regenerate (extraction scripts run, drift check), tribunal-smoke (Reference-vs-Reference differential returns clean), and — growing with each gate — judge-smoke (fln check-olean on a small fixture set), parity-smoke (a tiny corpus through T2). Every future subsystem bead extends this family rather than inventing parallel harnesses; test logging conventions (structured events, stable ids, no swallowed stderr) are established here once.

Acceptance: scripts/check.sh green locally and in CI; a seeded failure in each stage stops the pipeline with the failing stage named; e2e logs validate against the NDJSON schema and are archived as CI artifacts. Tests: self-test mode for the harness (planted failures produce correct exit codes and log records); log-schema contract test.

design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — this bead is the mandatory verification contract and scenario registry, not only a convenience script.

In addition to the existing scope, define a machine-checked verification manifest schema keyed by bead id, requirement/claim id, FL-INV id, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, workstream, and convergence/performance gate. Every implementation bead must register: deterministic unit tests for success/boundary/error/resource/cancel/failure-atomicity behavior; applicable property, metamorphic, fuzz, mutation-kill, and fault-drill coverage; at least one real user-path E2E scenario and one negative/recovery scenario; and the evidence artifacts that close the requirement. CI fails on an implemented row with no registered coverage, an orphan scenario, a release claim backed only by mocks, an unclassified skip, or a scenario that does not assert its final state.

The shared E2E runner must produce paired human and schema-versioned NDJSON logs containing run/scenario/step correlation ids, bead/claim/invariant/row/gate ids, redacted command argv, cwd, toolchain epoch, mode/profile, platform, thread count, seed, cache state, host facts, canonical input hashes, expected/actual values, monotonic timing, resource/budget facts, exit/signal, stdout/stderr artifact references, receipt/logical roots, first-divergence records, and cleanup/final-state assertions. Logs are bounded and secret-redacted, stderr is never swallowed, and CI archives everything needed to diagnose a failure without rerunning it.
acceptance_criteria: The verification manifest and log schemas are frozen with contract tests; every existing task bead has a registered verification obligation; seeded omissions (missing unit coverage, mock-only gate evidence, missing negative E2E, unclassified skip, swallowed stderr, absent final-state assertion, secret leakage, or malformed NDJSON) fail CI with the exact bead and requirement named. scripts/check.sh and the shared runner work locally and in CI, and a planted E2E failure is diagnosable from archived artifacts alone.

title: W1: Tribunal campaign machinery — mutation, fuzzing, drills, delta-debugger, thread matrix
description:
Build the Tribunal campaign machinery as a reusable framework (§18.2, §18.3) — the apparatus the per-subsystem rigs plug into, distinct from the W1 bootstrap (epoch lab + differential harness):

(1) The mutation-campaign framework: seeded-defect injection (skipped positivity check, inverted universe condition, leaked child-transaction assignment, omitted effect read, corrupted olean bounds check, dropped retain, bypassed capability, stale publication generation accepted, cache receipt trusted without hash validation) with the kill-tracking ledger — every critical mutant must be killed by a NAMED test or check; a surviving critical mutant blocks the gate. (2) The fuzzing fleet: grammar-based Lean source generation (syntax-directed + Corpus mutation), kernel term storms, region/olean structure fuzzing, VM opcode fuzzing — all under budgets, all crash-triaged to fixtures automatically. (3) The fault-and-recovery drill framework: kill -9 at every CAS/manifest promotion step, corrupted local/remote cache objects, disk-full mid-build, compiler-child crashes, server crashes with coordinator restart — each drill declaring an EXPECTED FINAL STATE and proving which products committed and which rolled back ("the process restarted" is not a pass). (4) The delta-debugger: automatic shrinking of any divergence or crash into a minimal checked-in fixture. (5) The no-mock lane enforcement: release-level claims close only against real binaries, real filesystems, real editors, real corruption — the evidence gate rejects mocked boundaries. (6) The thread-matrix determinism runner: {1,8,32} per commit, {96}+ weekly, diffing environments/diagnostics/artifacts bit-for-bit (PG-5 infrastructure).

Acceptance: each framework demonstrated on a seeded target with detailed structured logging; the kill-ledger, drill-expectation, and no-mock policies are machine-checked in CI, not conventions. Tests: framework self-tests (planted mutant survives -> gate blocks; drill final-state mismatch -> failure with the state diff logged).

title: W2: olean-next + fln olean tool — content-addressed modules, sealed capsules, diffs
description:
Implement olean-next + the fln olean tool (§7.4, frontier): the second module lane — a content-addressed, position-independent format: per-declaration records keyed by Ledger hashes, structure-shared term DAGs with dictionary compression, mergeable and diffable, mmap-friendly WITHOUT relocation, carrying certificate attachments (§8.6) inline. olean-next never replaces olean in faithful/sound artifacts (D-08); it is the Ledger native interchange and the format proof-cache federation speaks.

Capsule shapes: THIN (references into the shared CAS) and SEALED (a complete transitive object pack, verifiable on an air-gapped machine — the archival/audit form, and the .flnpack the WASM Judge consumes). Attested evidence objects may carry RaptorQ repair sidecars via the asupersync coder; repair reconstructs to staging, re-verifies the canonical hash and a full schema decode, publishes without changing identity, and journals the corruption/repair event — repair is a durability feature, NEVER a trust shortcut. The fln olean CLI tool converts both directions (olean <-> olean-next) and prints human-readable diffs of module contents — the un-opaquing of weakness census #8 (pointer-dump oleans are unmergeable, un-diffable, un-addressable).

Acceptance: round-trip olean -> olean-next -> olean preserves semantic hash and (faithful path) bytes; sealed capsules verify air-gapped (no CAS access); repair-sidecar loss and corruption drills pass with journaled events; diff output validated on known module pairs. Tests: format fuzzing under budgets; thin/sealed equivalence property tests; a tamper test (any flipped byte fails verification with a typed error naming the object); dictionary-compression determinism (same input, same bytes).

title: W12: Front doors — lean/leanc/lake personalities + the fln multiplexer verbs
description:
Implement the toolchain front doors (§17.1, fln-cli): the binaries lean, leanc (the C-driver personality: flag-compatible, driving the D2 cc when present, explaining its absence via capability errors when not), lake, and the fln multiplexer exposing the new verbs — check-olean, audit (--tcb), replay, doctor (--sql), cache, olean, goals, serve-mcp, why-trusts, diff, build explain, server replay, verify-capsule, identity.

Contract: flags, exit codes, env vars (LEAN_PATH, LEAN_SYSROOT), and --json shapes pinned to the Reference at the pin (CLI transcript corpus, C-family fixtures); --trust levels ACCEPTED with upstream semantics but journaled into receipts and surfaced by fln audit rather than silently honored (BN-09); new flags live under --fln-* only; the version line stays parse-compatible (Reference version/commit fields preserved) with a separate machine-readable implementation field — compatibility never becomes deception, and an exact-reference transcript mode exists for conformance harnesses only. Robot-mode discipline per AGENTS.md: stable versioned schemas, deterministic where possible, explicit exit codes, line-oriented output, no human decoration mixed into machine streams.

Acceptance: the CLI transcript corpus diffs clean against the Reference for the shared surface; each fln verb has a --json schema contract test against a frozen fixture; elan invokes the binaries transparently via the release layout (verified in W12). Tests: golden transcripts per verb (human and JSON modes) with detailed logging of any drift; exit-code matrix fixtures; --trust journaling fixtures; capability-error fixtures for missing cc/git naming the alternative.

title: W12: fln library crate — the embeddable prover, capability-first API
description:
Implement the fln embeddable library crate (§17.2, the library door): embed the prover — parse, elaborate, check, query — in Rust programs with the SAME engine and the same guarantees as the toolchain binaries; verification inside build tools, CI services, and agents becomes a Cargo.toml line, the posture the Reference architecture makes awkward (process-spawning, stage0, C toolchain) and this one makes trivial.

API design laws: capability-first — an embedder hands the engine a Cx and receives determinism, cancellation, and budget enforcement STRUCTURALLY (no global state, no ambient authority; the embedder controls filesystem/network/clock exposure); typed errors per the D8 taxonomy crossing the API boundary as versioned values, never panics (a panic across the embedding boundary is an invariant failure); the toolchain binaries (fln-cli) are thin shells over this crate so the library IS the tested product, not a parallel implementation; epoch selection explicit (Engine::builder().toolchain_epoch(...)); results carry receipts and logical roots so embedders can persist and re-verify.

Acceptance: an example embedder (checked into examples/) parses, elaborates, and kernel-checks a small project and retrieves a receipt, under a restricted Cx that denies network — demonstrated in CI; fln-cli refactored to consume the library surface; API documented with the semver-per-epoch policy. Tests: embedding e2e test with detailed logging (each API call logged with inputs/outcomes); a panic-containment test (an induced internal fault surfaces as InternalFault, never unwinds across the boundary); Cx-denial tests (denied capabilities produce typed CapabilityDenied); thread-safety smoke under the lab runtime.

title: W1: UPSTREAM_LEDGER + suite upgrade protocol — deliberate, Tribunal-diffed lock bumps
description:
Establish UPSTREAM_LEDGER.md + the suite upgrade protocol (§2.11, R16): primitives that belong in a foundation crate land THERE, tracked per-entry — seed entries from the plan: asupersync (deterministic work-stealing merge combinator — ordered join of speculative results — if the existing quorum/pipeline combinators do not already express it), franken_networkx (incremental dominator maintenance for dynamic DAGs — the live-edit invalidation cone), frankensearch (incremental index commit hooks matching Ledger transaction boundaries), franken_markdown (the Lean lexer profile for the shared highlighter), frankensqlite (any blob-store access pattern the CAS proves out). fln-bignum stays in-repo (kernel-adjacent code answers to the kernel covenant) with the recorded revisit trigger: a second suite member needing arbitrary precision.

The suite upgrade protocol (R16 mitigation): SUITE.lock upgrades are deliberate and Tribunal-diffed — an upgrade PR must carry the closure-audit delta (any new transitive dep is a reviewed allowlist change, D1), the conformance run against the new pin, and a rollback note; per-component acceptance gates before a suite feature becomes load-bearing; if a needed component is immature under this load, the fallback is extracting the narrow algorithm into an fln-* crate (design-level reuse) or rejecting the component — the only-Franken rule holds.

Acceptance: UPSTREAM_LEDGER.md exists with the seed entries, statuses, and owning-repo links; the upgrade-protocol checklist is enforced by CI on any SUITE.lock diff (a lock change without the audit artifacts fails). Tests: the CI enforcement check itself (a seeded bare lock bump must fail with the missing-artifact list logged).

title: W10: native_decide Tier 2 — trace-guided kernel replay discharges ofReduceBool
description:
Implement native_decide Tier 2 — trace-guided kernel replay (§11.6): on certified paths, ofReduceBool is DISCHARGED, not trusted — the Tier 1 replay receipt (function-entry digests over the registered reflection set, branch decisions, literal-arithmetic transcripts) is replayed through Crucible as the DEFAULT for certified profiles, so the compiler and VM leave the trusted base for every evaluation the tier covers.

Scope and honesty rules: the tier holds only where the receipt covers the full evaluation (the registered reflection set; evaluations escaping it remain Tier 1 and are marked so); the trust ledger records per build which tier was achieved, because "we no longer trust the compiler" is a claim with a scope; the cost multiple of replay vs native evaluation is measured and published (an OBSERVED number, per §20.4 — never asserted); Tier 3 (a verified evaluator proven against the Judgment Specification) remains explicitly research and is NOT this bead. Certified --reproducible builds enable Tier 2 by default; standard builds keep Tier 1 sampling policy.

Acceptance: the reflection corpus discharges through kernel replay on certified profiles with zero trust extension to Golem/Iron for covered evaluations; the trust ledger and fln audit --tcb reflect the tier per declaration; the published cost-multiple table lands in the evidence bundle for G6. Tests: e2e certified-build test where a native_decide theorem verifies with the compiler deliberately perturbed (a wrong compiled result MUST be caught by replay — the whole point, proven by mutation); receipt-coverage boundary fixtures (an evaluation outside the registered set must degrade to Tier 1 with the degradation logged and marked, never silently claimed as Tier 2); replay determinism across the certified matrix.

title: W1: Performance-gate harness — bench binaries, baselines, tail gates, flame artifacts
description:
Build the performance-gate harness (§19.2, §19.1 discipline): the infrastructure every PG gate (PG-1 the Judge, PG-2 cold corpus, PG-3 the cone, PG-4 attach, PG-5 determinism, PG-6 memory, PG-7 Golem, PG-8 codec, PG-9 Verdict, PG-K bignum, PG-10 cache, PG-L latency, PG-1b consensus tax, PG-M the Mirror) plugs into — one harness, not fourteen ad-hoc scripts.

Per gate: a bench binary; a committed, versioned baseline; a variance budget; alert-first thresholds graduating to blocking after two releases of baseline (the manim discipline); regression gating on TAILS as well as medians (interactive p95 > 5% blocks; kernel-throughput > 5% blocks; build wall/RSS > 7% blocks); a flamegraph artifact automatically attached on regression identifying the bottleneck; a rollback path recorded for risky changes. Every benchmark bundle records host facts, isolation checks, cache state, sample counts, and RAW samples — runs failing host-quality checks are retained as invalid, never averaged in. Scaling profiles (parallel efficiency at {1,8,32,96} threads for elaboration-bound and check-bound workloads) publish per release. Every published number carries its §20.4 claim state: OBSERVED on a named corpus and machine, or TARGETED — documentation CI (W12 Witness) enforces that the README can never say "2x faster than Lean" off one warm-cache loop.

Detailed logging is constitutive: each bench run emits structured NDJSON (per-phase timings, host fingerprint, cache state, sample-by-sample results) archived as CI artifacts so any regression is diagnosable from artifacts alone. Acceptance: the harness runs the first real gate end-to-end (PG-8 or PG-K are the earliest candidates) against the W1 baseline with the full bundle emitted; a seeded regression trips the tail gate and attaches the flame artifact. Tests: harness self-test with planted regressions per threshold class; baseline-drift detection (a silently edited baseline fails CI); host-quality-check rejection fixtures; bundle-schema contract tests.

title: W1: Typed error taxonomy + faithful diagnostic renderers — D8 values, R9 epoch projection
description:
Implement the typed error taxonomy + the faithful diagnostic renderers (D8 normative taxonomy, risk R9): errors cross crate boundaries as TYPED, VERSIONED VALUES — SyntaxFailure, MacroFailure, ElaborationFailure, KernelRejection, KernelInconclusive (resources/cancellation, FL-INV-07), ArtifactCorrupt, ArtifactEpochMismatch, AbiViolation, CapabilityDenied, PluginCrashed, BuildFailure, ProtocolFailure, ReplayDivergence, InternalInvariantViolation — defined once in fln-core, consumed everywhere. Panics are invariant failures, never user diagnostics; malformed source, artifacts, protocol messages, and plugin output must not panic anywhere in the workspace.

The renderer half (R9: faithful diagnostic text/ordering can depend on incidental internal traversal order upstream): faithful-mode frontends render the typed values into Reference-compatible text and JSON via a per-EPOCH renderer — the typed causal model is the source of truth, the renderer is a projection, and receipts retain the typed cause even when the rendered text is upstream-identical. Message TEXT is equivalence-classed per §4.1 (the Parity Ledger carries separate semantic-vs-exact rows); where exact text depends on incidental upstream order, the row holds at L2/L3 (semantic) with the exact-renderer gap NAMED, and CGSE policies pin our own rendering order. Sound mode renders the richer Palimpsest bodies (BN-02) with positions and severities unchanged.

Acceptance: the taxonomy crate compiles with exhaustive match enforcement (adding a variant breaks every consumer until handled — no catch-all arms in authoritative crates); the faithful renderer reproduces Reference diagnostics on the transcript corpus at each row declared class (exact or semantic); every panic path in a fuzz sweep of malformed inputs surfaces as a typed value, never an abort. Tests: renderer golden tests against the C-family diagnostic transcript corpus with per-row comparison classes logged; a taxonomy-versioning test (an old serialized error decodes or fails typed, never garbles); panic-freedom fuzzing across every decoder entry point; equivalence-class regression tests so a wording change in sound mode can never leak into faithful output.

title: W1: Mode system + certified axis — structural faithful/sound/frontier, D18 leak prevention
description:
Implement the mode system + determinism/evidence axis plumbing (§4.2, D17, D18): the machinery that makes faithful / sound / frontier a STRUCTURAL property of every artifact rather than a flag convention.

Scope: mode selection threading through every subsystem (one type, one source of truth, no per-crate booleans); the orthogonal determinism axis — standard (FL-INV-01 always holds) vs certified (--reproducible: the complete content-hashed input closure — sources, toolchain, SUITE.lock, options, plugin hashes, mode, CGSE policy epoch — yields bit-identical artifacts across the certified matrix, with the sidecar closure manifest; D2 tool products excluded from certification BY CONSTRUCTION); the evidence axis wiring (per-surface L-levels and per-release R-levels read from the Parity Ledger, never asserted). The D18 leak-prevention law is the heart: frontier features NEVER leak into faithful/sound artifacts — every artifact carries its producing mode in provenance, faithful/sound artifact sets are CI-scanned for frontier products (a frontier-marked object in a sound artifact set is a build break), and frontier-only code paths (olean-next emission, e-graph lanes, Iron-JIT, MCP write-tools, epoch bridges) are gated at compile time or capability level, not by if-statements convention.

Why this is its own bead: a dozen other beads say "faithful mode does X, sound mode does Y, frontier never leaks" — this bead builds the mechanism they all assume, early enough that no subsystem invents its own mode plumbing. Acceptance: mode + axis types land in fln-core with the provenance-marking scheme; the leak-scan CI check catches a seeded frontier artifact in a sound set; a --reproducible fixture build produces the sidecar manifest and bit-identical outputs on two runs. Tests: mode-propagation property tests (no code path observes a mode other than the session mode); leak-scan mutation tests; certified-closure completeness test (omitting any closure input from the manifest fails); Behavior-Note linkage checks (every sound-vs-faithful divergence point names its BN id, CI-verified against the register).

title: W2: D2 subprocess capsule — the one sanctioned tool-spawn path, fully provenanced
description:
Implement the D2 subprocess protocol as one shared capsule (Rule D2): the single implementation through which the ONLY two external tools FrankenLean may ever spawn — the system C compiler (only from the --backend c code generator, mirroring upstream leanc) and system git (only from Lake-compatible dependency fetching, mirroring upstream Lake) — are invoked. Nothing else in the program spawns processes, ever (no cc at check time, no CaDiCaL, no curl, no tar, no LaTeX), and CI enforces that this capsule is the only process-spawn site outside the Tribunal sandbox.

The protocol, in full: argv-only invocation (no shell interpretation, ever); per-job private temp directories; timeouts with process-TREE cancellation (no orphaned grandchildren); output-size limits; environment allowlist (nothing ambient leaks in); atomic result publication (a killed job leaves staging, never a half-written product); and the executable path AND CONTENT HASH recorded into provenance so a build record names exactly which cc/git produced what. Absence of a tool yields a typed CapabilityDenied error NAMING the alternative (Iron for cc; atp/HTTPS CAS sync guidance for git) — never silent substitution. Products of both tools are excluded from --reproducible artifact certification by construction (D17), and the Reference toolchain is NOT a third tool: a release check proves no fln binary can locate, spawn, or link it (D8).

Acceptance: leanc --backend c and Lake require both route through the capsule with full provenance rows; the spawn-site CI scan finds exactly one call site; kill/timeout drills leave no orphan processes and no partial products; absent-tool fixtures produce the typed error with the named alternative. Tests: process-tree cancellation drills (grandchild spawned by cc must die with the job); env-allowlist tests (a canary env var must not reach the child); output-bomb tests (limit enforced, typed failure, no OOM); provenance-hash tests (swapping the cc binary changes the recorded content hash and invalidates dependent D4-class records).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. title: W5: Golem runtime integration — generated intrinsics, effects, plugins, G2 parity
description:
Integrate the production Golem execution stack after its independently testable components exist: validated FIR-to-FLBC output, the FLBC interpreter host-call ABI, the census-generated intrinsic table, Marrow allocation/RC, asupersync IO/Task effects, initializer ordering and capability receipts, and the inbound Reference-plugin loader. This bead owns the no-mock end-to-end proof of the §11 claim; component beads may prove their own stable interfaces without pretending that future components already exist.

Required behavior: compile and execute real user-class Lean code (mathlib-style tactics, project macros, lakefile-shaped configuration, #eval and IO/Task bodies) on ABI-valued registers; cross interpreted code <-> native intrinsics <-> Reference-built plugin calls with zero representation conversion; preserve initialization/extension ordering; count heartbeats through the same allocator hook; enforce typed budgets and cancellation; retain RC balance and ownership-shadow evidence; record capability and plugin provenance. No test stub, prototype intrinsic, or oracle artifact may satisfy production acceptance.

Acceptance: the G0-3 corpus is promoted to the complete production stack; #eval/IO/Task and plugin results match the Reference at each registered comparison class; PG-7 interpreter measurements are emitted against the W1 baseline; the C4 inbound matrix is green at honest per-platform L-levels; initialization-DAG and capability receipts are complete; two runs at each certified thread count produce identical logical results and evidence identities where required.

Verification: unit contract tests at every component seam plus shared no-mock E2E scripts for pure computation, IO/Task ordering, capability denial, cancellation, panic/partial guards, plugin load/run/crash, and RC/heartbeat accounting. Every scenario emits the mandatory human and NDJSON evidence with the first cross-boundary divergence and final ownership/obligation state.

title: W7: Engine-backed tactic facade integration — simp, norm_num, omega, bv_decide, grind
description:
Complete the engine-backed core-tactic surface after the W6 native tactic framework and W7 engines exist. Wire the stable facade/dispatch contracts to the faithful simp core, norm_num and omega certificate producers, Verdict bv_decide, and native grind; execute both direct core invocations and real Golem-hosted user tactics through CoreM/MetaM/TermElabM/TacticM; require every produced declaration to pass Crucible and the configured consensus policy (FL-INV-02/06).

This bead removes a stage-order contradiction: W6 owns goal/state/backtracking/InfoTree semantics and the bootstrap-safe primitive tactic set; W7 owns these engine-backed rows. Until this bead lands, the corresponding census rows remain at their honest L0/L1 state and return precise typed Unsupported results where invoked — no fake implementation, compatibility shim, or hidden oracle.

Acceptance: the metaprogram corpus exercises each engine-backed row through the production facade; environments, InfoTrees, diagnostics, generated names, tactic state/backtracking, and goal-closure behavior match the Reference at each row's declared comparison class; simp/grind decision traces replay; norm_num/omega/Verdict certificates replay through Crucible; unsupported markers for the completed rows disappear only when their evidence rows promote.

Verification: per-row unit and negative tests, backtracking/failure-atomicity properties, bogus-certificate and direct-admission mutation tests, budget/cancellation scenarios, and shared no-mock E2E scripts running real tactic files on Golem with detailed step, trace, receipt, and first-divergence logging.

title: W8: Publish compiled rewrite indexes — Ledger CAS + olean-next attachment integration
description:
Integrate W7's deterministic compiled rewrite-index format with the W8 Ledger and Grimoire artifact planes. Publish each per-library discrimination automaton as a typed demand-node product and olean-next attachment keyed by the exact simp-set semantic hash, implementation/schema version, epoch, mode, and CGSE policy; hydrate it through local/remote CAS validation; project it into daemon sessions without rebuilding; and invalidate or reject it structurally whenever any key input changes.

This bead resolves the stage boundary cleanly: W7 implements and proves the faithful simp driver plus deterministic index builder/reader equivalence; W8 owns transactional publication, retention, hydration, invalidation, quarantine, and cache provenance. No W7 component may claim a Ledger-published artifact before this integration exists.

Acceptance: a mathlib slice builds indexes once, publishes atomically, reloads from a fresh process and from a validated remote-cache fixture, and yields candidate sets and simp results identical to a fresh in-memory index; unchanged simp sets reuse byte-identical artifacts; changed rules/options/epoch/schema cannot load stale artifacts; promotion crashes leave no half-published attachment.

Verification: unit schema/key tests, builder-vs-loaded representation equivalence, stale-key and poisoned-cache mutations, kill-at-every-promotion-step drills, and shared no-mock E2E scripts for build -> publish -> hydrate -> simplify with complete CAS keys, manifests, hashes, receipts, expected/final states, and first-divergence logs.

title: W12: Epoch migration autopilot integration — audited repair, explicit apply, rollback
description:
Integrate the §13.6 migration autopilot at the stage where all of its real prerequisites exist: the W12 epoch ratchet and machine-diffed censuses, W8 epoch bridges/cache identities, W9 semantic diff and blame slices, and W11 Bloodhound's verified proof-repair engine. Audit an unchanged downstream project against the new epoch; locate the earliest failed constraints and affected surfaces; propose minimal source/config/import/termination/instance/simp repairs; run lockstep old/new builds; validate every candidate through Athanor, Crucible, and the configured consensus policy; and emit an explicit apply transaction with a sealed rollback bundle. It never edits a project without an explicit apply.

Issue-tracker integration must respect D2: shipped FrankenLean tooling never spawns br or any third external tool. The ratchet emits a deterministic, schema-versioned migration-issue manifest with one row per moved symbol/divergence and exact br import/apply instructions for the repository-maintenance harness or human operator; applying that manifest is outside release-binary execution and is separately receipted.

Acceptance: a synthetic epoch delta and at least one real historical Lean migration run end to end; unchanged projects that need no repair remain byte/semantic clean; repaired projects build under the new epoch with every patch kernel-checked; rejected or partially applied migrations restore exactly from the rollback root; unclassified divergences block publication; migration issue manifests are deterministic and complete.

Verification: unit tests for census-to-impact mapping and transaction preconditions; property tests for rollback identity/idempotent re-apply; repair-ranking and explicit-consent negatives; lockstep divergence fixtures; and shared no-mock E2E scripts covering audit -> propose -> verify -> preview -> explicit apply -> rollback, with detailed old/new roots, patch hashes, receipts, issue-manifest rows, commands, final states, and first-divergence logs.

title: Reconcile plan §15 '(fln-trace, fln-graph)' vs §21 crate map (no fln-graph crate)
description: Plan §15's section title names two crates — fln-trace, fln-graph — but the authoritative cycle-free crate map in §21 (and AGENTS.md's layering chain) lists only fln-trace; the workspace scaffold (bead fln-8mj) follows §21. Either §15's title is stale (Palimpsest's causal-graph store lives inside fln-trace on franken_networkx per §15.3c) and the plan text should drop fln-graph, or a real fln-graph crate is intended and must be added to §21's map + WORKSPACE_GRAPH.txt with a rank and charter. Per the spec-first workflow, amend the plan first, then the workspace. One-line decision; blocks nothing today.
